authors_text: Alejandro Peña,Ignacio Serna,Aythami Morales,Julian Fierrez,Alfonso Ortega,Ainhoa Herrarte,Manuel Alcantara,Javier Ortega-Garcia
title: Human-Centric Multimodal Machine Learning: Recent Advances and Testbed on AI-based Recruitment
title_zh: 以人为本的多模式机器学习：基于人工智能的招聘的最新进展和测试平台
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2004.07173
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2004.07173
abstract: The presence of decision-making algorithms in society is rapidly increasing nowadays, while concerns about their transparency and the possibility of these algorithms becoming new sources of discrimination are arising. There is a certain consensus about the need to develop AI applications with a Human-Centric approach. Human-Centric Machine Learning needs to be developed based on four main requirements: (i) utility and social good; (ii) privacy and data ownership; (iii) transparency and accountability; and (iv) fairness in AI-driven decision-making processes. All these four Human-Centric requirements are closely related to each other. With the aim of studying how current multimodal algorithms based on heterogeneous sources of information are affected by sensitive elements and inner biases in the data, we propose a fictitious case study focused on automated recruitment: FairCVtest. We train automatic recruitment algorithms using a set of multimodal synthetic profiles including image, text, and structured data, which are consciously scored with gender and racial biases. FairCVtest shows the capacity of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) behind automatic recruitment tools built this way (a common practice in many other application scenarios beyond recruitment) to extract sensitive information from unstructured data and exploit it in combination to data biases in undesirable (unfair) ways. We present an overview of recent works developing techniques capable of removing sensitive information and biases from the decision-making process of deep learning architectures, as well as commonly used databases for fairness research in AI. We demonstrate how learning approaches developed to guarantee privacy in latent spaces can lead to unbiased and fair automatic decision-making process.

We train automatic recruitment algorithms using a set of multimodal synthetic profiles including image, text, and structured data, which are consciously scored with gender and racial biases. FairCVtest shows the capacity of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) behind automatic recruitment tools built this way (a common practice in many other application scenarios beyond recruitment) to extract sensitive information from unstructured data and exploit it in combination to data biases in undesirable (unfair) ways. We present an overview of recent works developing techniques capable of removing sensitive information and biases from the decision-making process of deep learning architectures, as well as commonly used databases for fairness research in AI. We demonstrate how learning approaches developed to guarantee privacy in latent spaces can lead to unbiased and fair automatic decision-making process.
abstract_zh: 如今，决策算法在社会中的存在正在迅速增加，同时人们也开始担心它们的透明度以及这些算法可能成为新的歧视来源。对于需要以人为本的方法开发人工智能应用程序存在一定的共识。以人为本的机器学习需要根据四个主要要求来开发：（i）实用性和社会公益； (ii) 隐私和数据所有权； (三) 透明度和问责制； (iv) 人工智能驱动的决策过程的公平性。所有这四个以人为本的要求彼此密切相关。为了研究当前基于异构信息源的多模态算法如何受到数据中敏感元素和内部偏差的影响，我们提出了一个专注于自动招聘的虚构案例研究：FairCVtest。我们使用一组多模式综合资料（包括图像、文本和结构化数据）来训练自动招聘算法，这些资料有意识地根据性别和种族偏见进行评分。 FairCVtest 展示了以这种方式构建的自动招聘工具背后的人工智能 (AI) 的能力（这是招聘以外的许多其他应用场景中的常见做法），可以从非结构化数据中提取敏感信息，并以不良（不公平）的方式将其与数据偏差结合起来利用。我们概述了最近的工作开发技术，这些技术能够消除深度学习架构决策过程中的敏感信息和偏见，以及人工智能公平性研究的常用数据库。我们展示了为保证潜在空间隐私而开发的学习方法如何导致公正和公平的自动决策过程。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为FairCVtest的测试平台，研究基于多模态数据的AI招聘算法如何受数据中的敏感信息和偏见影响，并探讨缓解方法。
AI.motivation: 随着AI决策算法在社会中的广泛应用，其透明性、公平性及潜在歧视风险引发关注。需开发符合人类中心理念的AI，确保社会福祉、隐私、透明性和公平性。
AI.method: 构建包含图像、文本和结构化数据的多模态合成数据集，故意植入性别和种族偏见，训练招聘算法并分析其对敏感信息的提取能力；综述去偏技术及公平性研究数据库。
AI.result: 实验表明AI可从非结构化数据中提取敏感信息并与数据偏见结合，导致不公平决策；隐私保护的潜在空间学习方法可减少偏见。
AI.conclusion: 需重视AI决策的公平性，FairCVtest为多模态系统提供评估框架，强调通过技术手段消除偏见的必要性。

search_text: Human-Centric Multimodal Machine Learning: Recent Advances and Testbed on AI-based Recruitment 以人为本的多模式机器学习：基于人工智能的招聘的最新进展和测试平台 The presence of decision-making algorithms in society is rapidly increasing nowadays, while concerns about their transparency and the possibility of these algorithms becoming new sources of discrimination are arising. There is a certain consensus about the need to develop AI applications with a Human-Centric approach. Human-Centric Machine Learning needs to be developed based on four main requirements: (i) utility and social good; (ii) privacy and data ownership; (iii) transparency and accountability; and (iv) fairness in AI-driven decision-making processes. All these four Human-Centric requirements are closely related to each other. With the aim of studying how current multimodal algorithms based on heterogeneous sources of information are affected by sensitive elements and inner biases in the data, we propose a fictitious case study focused on automated recruitment: FairCVtest. We train automatic recruitment algorithms using a set of multimodal synthetic profiles including image, text, and structured data, which are consciously scored with gender and racial biases. FairCVtest shows the capacity of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) behind automatic recruitment tools built this way (a common practice in many other application scenarios beyond recruitment) to extract sensitive information from unstructured data and exploit it in combination to data biases in undesirable (unfair) ways. We present an overview of recent works developing techniques capable of removing sensitive information and biases from the decision-making process of deep learning architectures, as well as commonly used databases for fairness research in AI. We demonstrate how learning approaches developed to guarantee privacy in latent spaces can lead to unbiased and fair automatic decision-making process. 如今，决策算法在社会中的存在正在迅速增加，同时人们也开始担心它们的透明度以及这些算法可能成为新的歧视来源。对于需要以人为本的方法开发人工智能应用程序存在一定的共识。以人为本的机器学习需要根据四个主要要求来开发：（i）实用性和社会公益； (ii) 隐私和数据所有权； (三) 透明度和问责制； (iv) 人工智能驱动的决策过程的公平性。所有这四个以人为本的要求彼此密切相关。为了研究当前基于异构信息源的多模态算法如何受到数据中敏感元素和内部偏差的影响，我们提出了一个专注于自动招聘的虚构案例研究：FairCVtest。我们使用一组多模式综合资料（包括图像、文本和结构化数据）来训练自动招聘算法，这些资料有意识地根据性别和种族偏见进行评分。 FairCVtest 展示了以这种方式构建的自动招聘工具背后的人工智能 (AI) 的能力（这是招聘以外的许多其他应用场景中的常见做法），可以从非结构化数据中提取敏感信息，并以不良（不公平）的方式将其与数据偏差结合起来利用。我们概述了最近的工作开发技术，这些技术能够消除深度学习架构决策过程中的敏感信息和偏见，以及人工智能公平性研究的常用数据库。我们展示了为保证潜在空间隐私而开发的学习方法如何导致公正和公平的自动决策过程。 本文提出一个名为FairCVtest的测试平台，研究基于多模态数据的AI招聘算法如何受数据中的敏感信息和偏见影响，并探讨缓解方法。 Alejandro Peña,Ignacio Serna,Aythami Morales,Julian Fierrez,Alfonso Ortega,Ainhoa Herrarte,Manuel Alcantara,Javier Ortega-Garcia cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CY

authors_text: Zhiye Guo,Jian Liu,Yanli Wang,Mengrui Chen,Duolin Wang,Dong Xu,Jianlin Cheng
title: Diffusion Models in Bioinformatics: A New Wave of Deep Learning Revolution in Action
title_zh: 生物信息学中的扩散模型：深度学习革命的新浪潮正在发生
comment: 16 pages, 2 figures, 2 tables, first version of the manuscript
comments: 16 pages, 2 figures, 2 tables, first version of the manuscript
abstract: Denoising diffusion models have emerged as one of the most powerful generative models in recent years. They have achieved remarkable success in many fields, such as computer vision, natural language processing (NLP), and bioinformatics. Although there are a few excellent reviews on diffusion models and their applications in computer vision and NLP, there is a lack of an overview of their applications in bioinformatics. This review aims to provide a rather thorough overview of the applications of diffusion models in bioinformatics to aid their further development in bioinformatics and computational biology. We start with an introduction of the key concepts and theoretical foundations of three cornerstone diffusion modeling frameworks (denoising diffusion probabilistic models, noise-conditioned scoring networks, and stochastic differential equations), followed by a comprehensive description of diffusion models employed in the different domains of bioinformatics, including cryo-EM data enhancement, single-cell data analysis, protein design and generation, drug and small molecule design, and protein-ligand interaction. The review is concluded with a summary of the potential new development and applications of diffusion models in bioinformatics.
summary: Denoising diffusion models have emerged as one of the most powerful generative models in recent years. They have achieved remarkable success in many fields, such as computer vision, natural language processing (NLP), and bioinformatics. Although there are a few excellent reviews on diffusion models and their applications in computer vision and NLP, there is a lack of an overview of their applications in bioinformatics. This review aims to provide a rather thorough overview of the applications of diffusion models in bioinformatics to aid their further development in bioinformatics and computational biology. We start with an introduction of the key concepts and theoretical foundations of three cornerstone diffusion modeling frameworks (denoising diffusion probabilistic models, noise-conditioned scoring networks, and stochastic differential equations), followed by a comprehensive description of diffusion models employed in the different domains of bioinformatics, including cryo-EM data enhancement, single-cell data analysis, protein design and generation, drug and small molecule design, and protein-ligand interaction. The review is concluded with a summary of the potential new development and applications of diffusion models in bioinformatics.
abstract_zh: 近年来，去噪扩散模型已成为最强大的生成模型之一。他们在计算机视觉、自然语言处理（NLP）和生物信息学等许多领域取得了令人瞩目的成功。尽管有一些关于扩散模型及其在计算机视觉和自然语言处理中的应用的优秀评论，但缺乏对其在生物信息学中的应用的概述。本综述旨在对扩散模型在生物信息学中的应用提供相当全面的概述，以帮助其在生物信息学和计算生物学中的进一步发展。我们首先介绍三个基石扩散建模框架（去噪扩散概率模型、噪声条件评分网络和随机微分方程）的关键概念和理论基础，然后全面描述生物信息学不同领域中使用的扩散模型，包括冷冻电镜数据增强、单细胞数据分析、蛋白质设计和生成、药物和小分子设计以及蛋白质-配体相互作用。综述最后总结了生物信息学中扩散模型的潜在新发展和应用。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了扩散模型在生物信息学中的应用，旨在推动其在生物信息学和计算生物学中的进一步发展。
AI.motivation: 尽管扩散模型在计算机视觉和自然语言处理领域已有综述，但其在生物信息学中的应用仍缺乏系统性总结，需填补这一空白以促进相关研究。
AI.method: 介绍三种核心扩散模型框架（去噪扩散概率模型、噪声条件评分网络、随机微分方程），并详细描述其在冷冻电镜数据增强、单细胞数据分析、蛋白质设计、药物分子设计及蛋白质-配体相互作用等领域的应用。
AI.result: 系统梳理了扩散模型在生物信息学各领域的应用现状，并总结了其未来潜在的发展方向和应用场景。
AI.conclusion: 扩散模型在生物信息学中展现出广阔前景，未来需进一步探索其在复杂生物数据生成与分析中的创新应用。

authors_text: Andrei Stoian,Jordan Frery,Roman Bredehoft,Luis Montero,Celia Kherfallah,Benoit Chevallier-Mames
title: Deep Neural Networks for Encrypted Inference with TFHE
title_zh: 使用 TFHE 进行加密推理的深度神经网络
abstract: Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) is an encryption method that allows to perform computation on encrypted data, without decryption. FHE preserves the privacy of the users of online services that handle sensitive data, such as health data, biometrics, credit scores and other personal information. A common way to provide a valuable service on such data is through machine learning and, at this time, Neural Networks are the dominant machine learning model for unstructured data. In this work we show how to construct Deep Neural Networks (DNN) that are compatible with the constraints of TFHE, an FHE scheme that allows arbitrary depth computation circuits. We discuss the constraints and show the architecture of DNNs for two computer vision tasks. We benchmark the architectures using the Concrete stack, an open-source implementation of TFHE.
summary: Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) is an encryption method that allows to perform computation on encrypted data, without decryption. FHE preserves the privacy of the users of online services that handle sensitive data, such as health data, biometrics, credit scores and other personal information. A common way to provide a valuable service on such data is through machine learning and, at this time, Neural Networks are the dominant machine learning model for unstructured data. In this work we show how to construct Deep Neural Networks (DNN) that are compatible with the constraints of TFHE, an FHE scheme that allows arbitrary depth computation circuits. We discuss the constraints and show the architecture of DNNs for two computer vision tasks. We benchmark the architectures using the Concrete stack, an open-source implementation of TFHE.
abstract_zh: 完全同态加密 (FHE) 是一种无需解密即可对加密数据执行计算的加密方法。 FHE 保护处理敏感数据（例如健康数据、生物识别、信用评分和其他个人信息）的在线服务用户的隐私。对此类数据提供有价值的服务的一种常见方法是通过机器学习，目前，神经网络是非结构化数据的主要机器学习模型。在这项工作中，我们展示了如何构建与 TFHE 约束兼容的深度神经网络 (DNN)，TFHE 是一种允许任意深度计算电路的 FHE 方案。我们讨论了约束条件并展示了两个计算机视觉任务的 DNN 架构。我们使用 Concrete 堆栈（TFHE 的开源实现）对架构进行基准测试。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何构建与TFHE兼容的深度神经网络，以实现加密数据上的推理计算。
AI.motivation: 全同态加密（FHE）能保护敏感数据隐私，但现有神经网络难以适配其计算约束，需探索可行方案。
AI.method: 设计符合TFHE计算深度限制的DNN架构，针对计算机视觉任务进行优化，并基于Concrete开源库进行性能验证。
AI.result: 提出了两种适配TFHE的DNN架构，并通过实验验证了其在加密推理中的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为在FHE环境下部署神经网络提供了有效方法，推动了隐私保护型AI的发展。

search_text: Deep Neural Networks for Encrypted Inference with TFHE 使用 TFHE 进行加密推理的深度神经网络 Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) is an encryption method that allows to perform computation on encrypted data, without decryption. FHE preserves the privacy of the users of online services that handle sensitive data, such as health data, biometrics, credit scores and other personal information. A common way to provide a valuable service on such data is through machine learning and, at this time, Neural Networks are the dominant machine learning model for unstructured data. In this work we show how to construct Deep Neural Networks (DNN) that are compatible with the constraints of TFHE, an FHE scheme that allows arbitrary depth computation circuits. We discuss the constraints and show the architecture of DNNs for two computer vision tasks. We benchmark the architectures using the Concrete stack, an open-source implementation of TFHE. 完全同态加密 (FHE) 是一种无需解密即可对加密数据执行计算的加密方法。 FHE 保护处理敏感数据（例如健康数据、生物识别、信用评分和其他个人信息）的在线服务用户的隐私。对此类数据提供有价值的服务的一种常见方法是通过机器学习，目前，神经网络是非结构化数据的主要机器学习模型。在这项工作中，我们展示了如何构建与 TFHE 约束兼容的深度神经网络 (DNN)，TFHE 是一种允许任意深度计算电路的 FHE 方案。我们讨论了约束条件并展示了两个计算机视觉任务的 DNN 架构。我们使用 Concrete 堆栈（TFHE 的开源实现）对架构进行基准测试。 本文研究如何构建与TFHE兼容的深度神经网络，以实现加密数据上的推理计算。 Andrei Stoian,Jordan Frery,Roman Bredehoft,Luis Montero,Celia Kherfallah,Benoit Chevallier-Mames cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CR

authors_text: Ruiyang Chen,Yingheng Tang,Jianzhu Ma,Weilu Gao
title: Scientific Computing with Diffractive Optical Neural Networks
title_zh: 使用衍射光神经网络进行科学计算
abstract: Diffractive optical neural networks (DONNs) have been emerging as a high-throughput and energy-efficient hardware platform to perform all-optical machine learning (ML) in machine vision systems. However, the current demonstrated applications of DONNs are largely straightforward image classification tasks, which undermines the prospect of developing and utilizing such hardware for other ML applications. Here, we numerically and experimentally demonstrate the deployment of an all-optical reconfigurable DONNs system for scientific computing, including guiding two-dimensional quantum material synthesis, predicting the properties of nanomaterials and small molecular cancer drugs, predicting the device response of nanopatterned integrated photonic power splitters, and the dynamic stabilization of an inverted pendulum with reinforcement learning. Despite a large variety of input data structures, we develop a universal feature engineering approach to convert categorical input features to the images that can be processed in the DONNs system. Our results open up new opportunities of employing DONNs systems for a broad range of ML applications.
summary: Diffractive optical neural networks (DONNs) have been emerging as a high-throughput and energy-efficient hardware platform to perform all-optical machine learning (ML) in machine vision systems. However, the current demonstrated applications of DONNs are largely straightforward image classification tasks, which undermines the prospect of developing and utilizing such hardware for other ML applications. Here, we numerically and experimentally demonstrate the deployment of an all-optical reconfigurable DONNs system for scientific computing, including guiding two-dimensional quantum material synthesis, predicting the properties of nanomaterials and small molecular cancer drugs, predicting the device response of nanopatterned integrated photonic power splitters, and the dynamic stabilization of an inverted pendulum with reinforcement learning. Despite a large variety of input data structures, we develop a universal feature engineering approach to convert categorical input features to the images that can be processed in the DONNs system. Our results open up new opportunities of employing DONNs systems for a broad range of ML applications.
abstract_zh: 衍射光学神经网络 (DONN) 已成为一种高吞吐量且节能的硬件平台，用于在机器视觉系统中执行全光学机器学习 (ML)。然而，当前展示的 DONN 应用主要是简单的图像分类任务，这削弱了为其他机器学习应用开发和利用此类硬件的前景。在这里，我们通过数值和实验演示了用于科学计算的全光学可重构 DONNs 系统的部署，包括指导二维量子材料合成、预测纳米材料和小分子癌症药物的特性、预测纳米图案集成光子功率分配器的器件响应以及通过强化学习实现倒立摆的动态稳定。尽管输入数据结构多种多样，但我们开发了一种通用的特征工程方法，将分类输入特征转换为可以在 DONNs 系统中处理的图像。我们的结果为将 DONN 系统用于广泛的机器学习应用开辟了新的机会。

AI.tldr: 展示衍射光学神经网络（DONNs）在科学计算中的应用，拓展其在机器学习领域的用途。
AI.motivation: 现有DONN应用局限于简单图像分类，限制其在更复杂科学计算中的应用，需拓展其功能。
AI.method: 开发通用特征工程方法，将各类输入数据转化为DONNs可处理的图像，并进行实验验证。
AI.result: 成功应用于量子材料合成、纳米材料性质预测、光子器件响应预测及倒立摆控制，验证了DONNs在复杂科学计算中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 证明DONNs可处理多样化的科学计算任务，为全光计算在机器学习中的广泛应用奠定基础。

authors_text: Farzan Shenavarmasouleh,Farid Ghareh Mohammadi,Khaled M. Rasheed,Hamid R. Arabnia
title: Deep Learning in Healthcare: An In-Depth Analysis
title_zh: 医疗保健中的深度学习：深入分析
comment: Full version of the accepted paper in The 8th International Conference on Health Informatics & Medical Systems (HIMS'22)
comments: Full version of the accepted paper in The 8th International Conference on Health Informatics & Medical Systems (HIMS'22)
abstract: Deep learning (DL) along with never-ending advancements in computational processing and cloud technologies have bestowed us powerful analyzing tools and techniques in the past decade and enabled us to use and apply them in various fields of study. Health informatics is not an exception, and conversely, is the discipline that generates the most amount of data in today's era and can benefit from DL the most. Extracting features and finding complex patterns from a huge amount of raw data and transforming them into knowledge is a challenging task. Besides, various DL architectures have been proposed by researchers throughout the years to tackle different problems. In this paper, we provide a review of DL models and their broad application in bioinformatics and healthcare categorized by their architecture. In addition, we also go over some of the key challenges that still exist and can show up while conducting DL research.
summary: Deep learning (DL) along with never-ending advancements in computational processing and cloud technologies have bestowed us powerful analyzing tools and techniques in the past decade and enabled us to use and apply them in various fields of study. Health informatics is not an exception, and conversely, is the discipline that generates the most amount of data in today's era and can benefit from DL the most. Extracting features and finding complex patterns from a huge amount of raw data and transforming them into knowledge is a challenging task. Besides, various DL architectures have been proposed by researchers throughout the years to tackle different problems. In this paper, we provide a review of DL models and their broad application in bioinformatics and healthcare categorized by their architecture. In addition, we also go over some of the key challenges that still exist and can show up while conducting DL research.
abstract_zh: 在过去的十年中，深度学习（DL）以及计算处理和云技术的不断进步为我们提供了强大的分析工具和技术，并使我们能够在各个研究领域中使用和应用它们。健康信息学也不例外，相反，它是当今时代生成数据量最多、最能从深度学习中受益的学科。从大量原始数据中提取特征并找到复杂模式并将其转化为知识是一项具有挑战性的任务。此外，多年来研究人员提出了各种深度学习架构来解决不同的问题。在本文中，我们回顾了深度学习模型及其在生物信息学和医疗保健中的广泛应用（按其架构进行分类）。此外，我们还回顾了一些仍然存在并且在进行深度学习研究时可能出现的关键挑战。

AI.tldr: 综述深度学习模型在生物信息学和医疗健康领域的应用，并按架构分类讨论
AI.motivation: 随着医疗数据的激增，利用深度学习处理复杂模式和提取特征成为挑战，需系统分析现有模型及挑战
AI.method: 通过回顾和分类不同深度学习架构在医疗领域的应用，分析其解决的问题及现有挑战
AI.result: 总结了多种深度学习架构在医疗健康中的具体应用，识别出当前研究中的主要挑战和未解决问题
AI.conclusion: 深度学习在医疗健康有巨大潜力，但需克服数据质量、模型可解释性及跨领域协作等挑战以推动实际应用

authors_text: Wei Chen,Chao Huang,Yanwei Yu,Yongguo Jiang,Junyu Dong
title: Trajectory-User Linking via Hierarchical Spatio-Temporal Attention Networks
title_zh: 通过分层时空注意力网络进行轨迹-用户链接
comment: 22 pages, 8 figures, accepted by ACM Trans. Knowl. Discov. Data Journal (TKDD)
comments: 22 pages, 8 figures, accepted by ACM Trans. Knowl. Discov. Data Journal (TKDD)
abstract: Trajectory-User Linking (TUL) is crucial for human mobility modeling by linking diferent trajectories to users with the exploration of complex mobility patterns. Existing works mainly rely on the recurrent neural framework to encode the temporal dependencies in trajectories, have fall short in capturing spatial-temporal global context for TUL prediction. To ill this gap, this work presents a new hierarchical spatio-temporal attention neural network, called AttnTUL, to jointly encode the local trajectory transitional patterns and global spatial dependencies for TUL. Speciically, our irst model component is built over the graph neural architecture to preserve the local and global context and enhance the representation paradigm of geographical regions and user trajectories. Additionally, a hierarchically structured attention network is designed to simultaneously encode the intra-trajectory and inter-trajectory dependencies, with the integration of the temporal attention mechanism and global elastic attentional encoder. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of our AttnTUL method as compared to state-of-the-art baselines on various trajectory datasets. The source code of our model is available at https://github.com/Onedean/AttnTUL.
summary: Trajectory-User Linking (TUL) is crucial for human mobility modeling by linking diferent trajectories to users with the exploration of complex mobility patterns. Existing works mainly rely on the recurrent neural framework to encode the temporal dependencies in trajectories, have fall short in capturing spatial-temporal global context for TUL prediction. To ill this gap, this work presents a new hierarchical spatio-temporal attention neural network, called AttnTUL, to jointly encode the local trajectory transitional patterns and global spatial dependencies for TUL. Speciically, our irst model component is built over the graph neural architecture to preserve the local and global context and enhance the representation paradigm of geographical regions and user trajectories. Additionally, a hierarchically structured attention network is designed to simultaneously encode the intra-trajectory and inter-trajectory dependencies, with the integration of the temporal attention mechanism and global elastic attentional encoder. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of our AttnTUL method as compared to state-of-the-art baselines on various trajectory datasets. The source code of our model is available at https://github.com/Onedean/AttnTUL.
abstract_zh: 轨迹-用户链接 (TUL) 通过将不同的轨迹与用户联系起来并探索复杂的移动模式，对于人类移动建模至关重要。现有的工作主要依靠循环神经框架来编码轨迹中的时间依赖性，在捕获 TUL 预测的时空全局上下文方面存在不足。为了弥补这一差距，这项工作提出了一种新的分层时空注意力神经网络，称为 AttnTUL，以联合编码 TUL 的局部轨迹转换模式和全局空间依赖性。具体来说，我们的第一个模型组件是建立在图神经架构之上的，以保留本地和全球上下文并增强地理区域和用户轨迹的表示范式。此外，还设计了一个分层结构的注意力网络，通过集成时间注意力机制和全局弹性注意力编码器，同时对轨迹内和轨迹间依赖性进行编码。大量实验证明了我们的 AttnTUL 方法与各种轨迹数据集上最先进的基线相比的优越性。我们模型的源代码可在 https://github.com/Oneedean/AttnTUL 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于分层时空注意力网络的方法AttnTUL，用于解决轨迹-用户链接问题。
AI.motivation: 现有的方法主要依赖于循环神经网络框架来编码轨迹中的时间依赖性，但在捕捉轨迹链接任务中的时空全局上下文方面存在不足。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种新的分层时空注意力神经网络AttnTUL，结合图神经网络结构和分层注意力网络，同时编码轨迹内的依赖关系和轨迹间的依赖关系。
AI.result: 在多个轨迹数据集上的实验表明，AttnTUL方法优于现有的先进基线方法。
AI.conclusion: AttnTUL方法在轨迹-用户链接任务中表现出色，能够有效捕捉时空全局上下文和轨迹依赖关系。

search_text: Trajectory-User Linking via Hierarchical Spatio-Temporal Attention Networks 通过分层时空注意力网络进行轨迹-用户链接 Trajectory-User Linking (TUL) is crucial for human mobility modeling by linking diferent trajectories to users with the exploration of complex mobility patterns. Existing works mainly rely on the recurrent neural framework to encode the temporal dependencies in trajectories, have fall short in capturing spatial-temporal global context for TUL prediction. To ill this gap, this work presents a new hierarchical spatio-temporal attention neural network, called AttnTUL, to jointly encode the local trajectory transitional patterns and global spatial dependencies for TUL. Speciically, our irst model component is built over the graph neural architecture to preserve the local and global context and enhance the representation paradigm of geographical regions and user trajectories. Additionally, a hierarchically structured attention network is designed to simultaneously encode the intra-trajectory and inter-trajectory dependencies, with the integration of the temporal attention mechanism and global elastic attentional encoder. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of our AttnTUL method as compared to state-of-the-art baselines on various trajectory datasets. The source code of our model is available at https://github.com/Onedean/AttnTUL. 轨迹-用户链接 (TUL) 通过将不同的轨迹与用户联系起来并探索复杂的移动模式，对于人类移动建模至关重要。现有的工作主要依靠循环神经框架来编码轨迹中的时间依赖性，在捕获 TUL 预测的时空全局上下文方面存在不足。为了弥补这一差距，这项工作提出了一种新的分层时空注意力神经网络，称为 AttnTUL，以联合编码 TUL 的局部轨迹转换模式和全局空间依赖性。具体来说，我们的第一个模型组件是建立在图神经架构之上的，以保留本地和全球上下文并增强地理区域和用户轨迹的表示范式。此外，还设计了一个分层结构的注意力网络，通过集成时间注意力机制和全局弹性注意力编码器，同时对轨迹内和轨迹间依赖性进行编码。大量实验证明了我们的 AttnTUL 方法与各种轨迹数据集上最先进的基线相比的优越性。我们模型的源代码可在 https://github.com/Oneedean/AttnTUL 获取。 本文提出了一种基于分层时空注意力网络的方法AttnTUL，用于解决轨迹-用户链接问题。 Wei Chen,Chao Huang,Yanwei Yu,Yongguo Jiang,Junyu Dong cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Maksims Kazijevs,Manar D. Samad
title: Deep Imputation of Missing Values in Time Series Health Data: A Review with Benchmarking
title_zh: 时间序列健康数据中缺失值的深度插补：基准审查
abstract: The imputation of missing values in multivariate time series (MTS) data is critical in ensuring data quality and producing reliable data-driven predictive models. Apart from many statistical approaches, a few recent studies have proposed state-of-the-art deep learning methods to impute missing values in MTS data. However, the evaluation of these deep methods is limited to one or two data sets, low missing rates, and completely random missing value types. This survey performs six data-centric experiments to benchmark state-of-the-art deep imputation methods on five time series health data sets. Our extensive analysis reveals that no single imputation method outperforms the others on all five data sets. The imputation performance depends on data types, individual variable statistics, missing value rates, and types. Deep learning methods that jointly perform cross-sectional (across variables) and longitudinal (across time) imputations of missing values in time series data yield statistically better data quality than traditional imputation methods. Although computationally expensive, deep learning methods are practical given the current availability of high-performance computing resources, especially when data quality and sample size are highly important in healthcare informatics. Our findings highlight the importance of data-centric selection of imputation methods to optimize data-driven predictive models.
summary: The imputation of missing values in multivariate time series (MTS) data is critical in ensuring data quality and producing reliable data-driven predictive models. Apart from many statistical approaches, a few recent studies have proposed state-of-the-art deep learning methods to impute missing values in MTS data. However, the evaluation of these deep methods is limited to one or two data sets, low missing rates, and completely random missing value types. This survey performs six data-centric experiments to benchmark state-of-the-art deep imputation methods on five time series health data sets. Our extensive analysis reveals that no single imputation method outperforms the others on all five data sets. The imputation performance depends on data types, individual variable statistics, missing value rates, and types. Deep learning methods that jointly perform cross-sectional (across variables) and longitudinal (across time) imputations of missing values in time series data yield statistically better data quality than traditional imputation methods. Although computationally expensive, deep learning methods are practical given the current availability of high-performance computing resources, especially when data quality and sample size are highly important in healthcare informatics. Our findings highlight the importance of data-centric selection of imputation methods to optimize data-driven predictive models.
abstract_zh: 多元时间序列 (MTS) 数据中缺失值的插补对于确保数据质量和生成可靠的数据驱动的预测模型至关重要。除了许多统计方法之外，最近的一些研究提出了最先进的深度学习方法来估算 MTS 数据中的缺失值。然而，这些深度方法的评估仅限于一两个数据集，缺失率较低，且缺失值类型完全随机。这项调查进行了六项以数据为中心的实验，以对五个时间序列健康数据集上最先进的深度插补方法进行基准测试。我们的广泛分析表明，没有任何一种插补方法在所有五个数据集上都优于其他方法。插补性能取决于数据类型、单个变量统计量、缺失值率和类型。深度学习方法联合执行时间序列数据中缺失值的横截面（跨变量）和纵向（跨时间）插补，在统计上比传统插补方法产生更好的数据质量。尽管计算成本昂贵，但考虑到当前高性能计算资源的可用性，深度学习方法是实用的，特别是当数据质量和样本量在医疗信息学中非常重要时。我们的研究结果强调了以数据为中心选择插补方法以优化数据驱动的预测模型的重要性。

AI.tldr: 本文综述并基准测试了用于时间序列健康数据缺失值插补的深度学习方法。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习插补方法的评估局限于少量数据集、低缺失率和单一缺失类型，缺乏全面比较。
AI.method: 在五个时间序列健康数据集上进行六项数据驱动实验，对比多种深度插补方法的性能差异。
AI.result: 无单一方法在所有数据集上表现最优，结合横断面与纵向插补的深度方法在数据质量上优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 应根据数据特征选择插补方法，尽管计算成本较高，但深度学习在医疗数据质量优化中具有实践价值。

authors_text: Zahra Jourahmad,Jafar Mehvari Habibabadi,Houshang Moein,Reza Basiratnia,Ali Rahmani Geranqayeh,Saeed Shiry Ghidary,Seyed-Ali Sadegh-Zadeh
title: Machine Learning Techniques for Predicting the Short-Term Outcome of Resective Surgery in Lesional-Drug Resistance Epilepsy
title_zh: 用于预测病变耐药性癫痫切除手术短期结果的机器学习技术
abstract: In this study, we developed and tested machine learning models to predict epilepsy surgical outcome using noninvasive clinical and demographic data from patients. Methods: Seven dif-ferent categorization algorithms were used to analyze the data. The techniques are also evaluated using the Leave-One-Out method. For precise evaluation of the results, the parameters accuracy, precision, recall and, F1-score are calculated. Results: Our findings revealed that a machine learning-based presurgical model of patients' clinical features may accurately predict the outcome of epilepsy surgery in patients with drug-resistant lesional epilepsy. The support vector machine (SVM) with the linear kernel yielded 76.1% in terms of accuracy could predict results in 96.7% of temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE) patients and 79.5% of extratemporal lobe epilepsy (ETLE) cases using ten clinical features. Significance: To predict the outcome of epilepsy surgery, this study recommends the use of a machine learning strategy based on supervised classification and se-lection of feature subsets data mining. Progress in the development of machine learning-based prediction models offers optimism for personalised medicine access.
summary: In this study, we developed and tested machine learning models to predict epilepsy surgical outcome using noninvasive clinical and demographic data from patients. Methods: Seven dif-ferent categorization algorithms were used to analyze the data. The techniques are also evaluated using the Leave-One-Out method. For precise evaluation of the results, the parameters accuracy, precision, recall and, F1-score are calculated. Results: Our findings revealed that a machine learning-based presurgical model of patients' clinical features may accurately predict the outcome of epilepsy surgery in patients with drug-resistant lesional epilepsy. The support vector machine (SVM) with the linear kernel yielded 76.1% in terms of accuracy could predict results in 96.7% of temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE) patients and 79.5% of extratemporal lobe epilepsy (ETLE) cases using ten clinical features. Significance: To predict the outcome of epilepsy surgery, this study recommends the use of a machine learning strategy based on supervised classification and se-lection of feature subsets data mining. Progress in the development of machine learning-based prediction models offers optimism for personalised medicine access.
abstract_zh: 在这项研究中，我们开发并测试了机器学习模型，以使用患者的无创临床和人口统计数据来预测癫痫手术结果。方法：使用七种不同的分类算法来分析数据。还使用留一法对这些技术进行评估。为了精确评估结果，计算参数准确度、精确度、召回率和 F1 分数。结果：我们的研究结果表明，基于机器学习的患者临床特征的术前模型可以准确预测耐药性病变癫痫患者的癫痫手术结果。具有线性核的支持向量机 (SVM) 的准确度为 76.1%，可以使用 10 种临床特征预测 96.7% 的颞叶癫痫 (TLE) 患者和 79.5% 的颞外叶癫痫 (ETLE) 病例的结果。意义：为了预测癫痫手术的结果，本研究建议使用基于监督分类和特征子集数据挖掘选择的机器学习策略。基于机器学习的预测模型的开发进展为个性化医疗提供了乐观的前景。

AI.tldr: 该论文旨在利用机器学习技术，基于非侵入性临床和人口统计数据预测难治性局灶性癫痫患者切除手术的短期结果。
AI.motivation: 癫痫手术的预后预测对患者选择和治疗计划至关重要，但传统方法存在局限性。因此，作者希望通过机器学习技术提高预测的准确性。
AI.method: 研究采用七种分类算法，并使用留一法进行评估。通过计算准确率、精确率、召回率和F1分数来评估模型性能。
AI.result: 研究发现，基于支持向量机（SVM）的线性核模型在使用10个临床特征时，对颞叶癫痫（TLE）患者的预测准确率为96.7%，对额叶外癫痫（ETLE）患者的预测准确率为79.5%。
AI.conclusion: 该研究推荐使用基于监督分类和特征子集选择的数据挖掘策略，以提高癫痫手术结果的预测能力，为个性化医疗提供希望。

ai_content_json.result: 研究发现，基于支持向量机（SVM）的线性核模型在使用10个临床特征时，对颞叶癫痫（TLE）患者的预测准确率为96.7%，对额叶外癫痫（ETLE）患者的预测准确率为79.5%。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 该研究推荐使用基于监督分类和特征子集选择的数据挖掘策略，以提高癫痫手术结果的预测能力，为个性化医疗提供希望。

tldr: 该论文旨在利用机器学习技术，基于非侵入性临床和人口统计数据预测难治性局灶性癫痫患者切除手术的短期结果。
motivation: 癫痫手术的预后预测对患者选择和治疗计划至关重要，但传统方法存在局限性。因此，作者希望通过机器学习技术提高预测的准确性。
method: 研究采用七种分类算法，并使用留一法进行评估。通过计算准确率、精确率、召回率和F1分数来评估模型性能。
result: 研究发现，基于支持向量机（SVM）的线性核模型在使用10个临床特征时，对颞叶癫痫（TLE）患者的预测准确率为96.7%，对额叶外癫痫（ETLE）患者的预测准确率为79.5%。
conclusion: 该研究推荐使用基于监督分类和特征子集选择的数据挖掘策略，以提高癫痫手术结果的预测能力，为个性化医疗提供希望。
search_text: Machine Learning Techniques for Predicting the Short-Term Outcome of Resective Surgery in Lesional-Drug Resistance Epilepsy 用于预测病变耐药性癫痫切除手术短期结果的机器学习技术 In this study, we developed and tested machine learning models to predict epilepsy surgical outcome using noninvasive clinical and demographic data from patients. Methods: Seven dif-ferent categorization algorithms were used to analyze the data. The techniques are also evaluated using the Leave-One-Out method. For precise evaluation of the results, the parameters accuracy, precision, recall and, F1-score are calculated. Results: Our findings revealed that a machine learning-based presurgical model of patients' clinical features may accurately predict the outcome of epilepsy surgery in patients with drug-resistant lesional epilepsy. The support vector machine (SVM) with the linear kernel yielded 76.1% in terms of accuracy could predict results in 96.7% of temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE) patients and 79.5% of extratemporal lobe epilepsy (ETLE) cases using ten clinical features. Significance: To predict the outcome of epilepsy surgery, this study recommends the use of a machine learning strategy based on supervised classification and se-lection of feature subsets data mining. Progress in the development of machine learning-based prediction models offers optimism for personalised medicine access. 在这项研究中，我们开发并测试了机器学习模型，以使用患者的无创临床和人口统计数据来预测癫痫手术结果。方法：使用七种不同的分类算法来分析数据。还使用留一法对这些技术进行评估。为了精确评估结果，计算参数准确度、精确度、召回率和 F1 分数。结果：我们的研究结果表明，基于机器学习的患者临床特征的术前模型可以准确预测耐药性病变癫痫患者的癫痫手术结果。具有线性核的支持向量机 (SVM) 的准确度为 76.1%，可以使用 10 种临床特征预测 96.7% 的颞叶癫痫 (TLE) 患者和 79.5% 的颞外叶癫痫 (ETLE) 病例的结果。意义：为了预测癫痫手术的结果，本研究建议使用基于监督分类和特征子集数据挖掘选择的机器学习策略。基于机器学习的预测模型的开发进展为个性化医疗提供了乐观的前景。 该论文旨在利用机器学习技术，基于非侵入性临床和人口统计数据预测难治性局灶性癫痫患者切除手术的短期结果。 Zahra Jourahmad,Jafar Mehvari Habibabadi,Houshang Moein,Reza Basiratnia,Ali Rahmani Geranqayeh,Saeed Shiry Ghidary,Seyed-Ali Sadegh-Zadeh cs.LG,cs.AI,q-bio.QM

authors_text: Liang Qu,Ningzhi Tang,Ruiqi Zheng,Quoc Viet Hung Nguyen,Zi Huang,Yuhui Shi,Hongzhi Yin
title: Semi-decentralized Federated Ego Graph Learning for Recommendation
title_zh: 用于推荐的半去中心化联邦自我图学习
abstract: Collaborative filtering (CF) based recommender systems are typically trained based on personal interaction data (e.g., clicks and purchases) that could be naturally represented as ego graphs. However, most existing recommendation methods collect these ego graphs from all users to compose a global graph to obtain high-order collaborative information between users and items, and these centralized CF recommendation methods inevitably lead to a high risk of user privacy leakage. Although recently proposed federated recommendation systems can mitigate the privacy problem, they either restrict the on-device local training to an isolated ego graph or rely on an additional third-party server to access other ego graphs resulting in a cumbersome pipeline, which is hard to work in practice. In addition, existing federated recommendation systems require resource-limited devices to maintain the entire embedding tables resulting in high communication costs. In light of this, we propose a semi-decentralized federated ego graph learning framework for on-device recommendations, named SemiDFEGL, which introduces new device-to-device collaborations to improve scalability and reduce communication costs and innovatively utilizes predicted interacted item nodes to connect isolated ego graphs to augment local subgraphs such that the high-order user-item collaborative information could be used in a privacy-preserving manner. Furthermore, the proposed framework is model-agnostic, meaning that it could be seamlessly integrated with existing graph neural network-based recommendation methods and privacy protection techniques. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed SemiDFEGL, extensive experiments are conducted on three public datasets, and the results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed SemiDFEGL compared to other federated recommendation methods.
summary: Collaborative filtering (CF) based recommender systems are typically trained based on personal interaction data (e.g., clicks and purchases) that could be naturally represented as ego graphs. However, most existing recommendation methods collect these ego graphs from all users to compose a global graph to obtain high-order collaborative information between users and items, and these centralized CF recommendation methods inevitably lead to a high risk of user privacy leakage. Although recently proposed federated recommendation systems can mitigate the privacy problem, they either restrict the on-device local training to an isolated ego graph or rely on an additional third-party server to access other ego graphs resulting in a cumbersome pipeline, which is hard to work in practice. In addition, existing federated recommendation systems require resource-limited devices to maintain the entire embedding tables resulting in high communication costs. In light of this, we propose a semi-decentralized federated ego graph learning framework for on-device recommendations, named SemiDFEGL, which introduces new device-to-device collaborations to improve scalability and reduce communication costs and innovatively utilizes predicted interacted item nodes to connect isolated ego graphs to augment local subgraphs such that the high-order user-item collaborative information could be used in a privacy-preserving manner. Furthermore, the proposed framework is model-agnostic, meaning that it could be seamlessly integrated with existing graph neural network-based recommendation methods and privacy protection techniques. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed SemiDFEGL, extensive experiments are conducted on three public datasets, and the results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed SemiDFEGL compared to other federated recommendation methods.
abstract_zh: 基于协作过滤（CF）的推荐系统通常是基于个人交互数据（例如点击和购买）进行训练的，这些数据可以自然地表示为自我图。然而，现有的推荐方法大多收集所有用户的自我图，组成全局图来获取用户和物品之间的高阶协作信息，而这些中心化的CF推荐方法不可避免地导致用户隐私泄露的高风险。尽管最近提出的联合推荐系统可以缓解隐私问题，但它们要么将设备上的本地训练限制为孤立的自我图，要么依赖额外的第三方服务器来访问其他自我图，从而导致繁琐的管道，这在实践中很难发挥作用。此外，现有的联合推荐系统需要资源有限的设备来维护整个嵌入表，从而导致高昂的通信成本。有鉴于此，我们提出了一种用于设备上推荐的半去中心化联合自我图学习框架，名为 SemiDFEGL，它引入了新的设备到设备协作来提高可扩展性并降低通信成本，并创新地利用预测的交互项目节点来连接孤立的自我图以增强本地子图，从而可以以保护隐私的方式使用高阶用户项目协作信息。此外，所提出的框架与模型无关，这意味着它可以与现有的基于图神经网络的推荐方法和隐私保护技术无缝集成。为了验证所提出的 SemiDFEGL 的有效性，在三个公共数据集上进行了广泛的实验，结果证明了所提出的 SemiDFEGL 与其他联合推荐方法相比的优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半去中心化的联邦自图学习框架SemiDFEGL，旨在解决推荐系统中用户隐私泄露问题并提升去中心化训练的效率。
AI.motivation: 传统协同过滤推荐系统因集中式训练存在隐私泄露风险，而现有联邦推荐方法受限于设备孤立训练或依赖第三方服务器，导致通信成本高且难以实际应用。
AI.method: 通过设备间协作连接孤立的自图，利用预测的交互物品节点增强局部子图，并采用模型无关设计兼容现有图神经网络与隐私保护技术。
AI.result: 在三个公开数据集上的实验表明，SemiDFEGL在推荐性能上优于其他联邦推荐方法，同时降低了通信成本。
AI.conclusion: 该框架在隐私保护、可扩展性和通信效率之间取得平衡，为实际部署联邦推荐系统提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Zhijian Li,Biao Yang,Penghang Yin,Yingyong Qi,Jack Xin
title: Feature Affinity Assisted Knowledge Distillation and Quantization of Deep Neural Networks on Label-Free Data
title_zh: 无标签数据上深度神经网络的特征亲和力辅助知识蒸馏和量化
abstract: In this paper, we propose a feature affinity (FA) assisted knowledge distillation (KD) method to improve quantization-aware training of deep neural networks (DNN). The FA loss on intermediate feature maps of DNNs plays the role of teaching middle steps of a solution to a student instead of only giving final answers in the conventional KD where the loss acts on the network logits at the output level. Combining logit loss and FA loss, we found that the quantized student network receives stronger supervision than from the labeled ground-truth data. The resulting FAQD is capable of compressing model on label-free data, which brings immediate practical benefits as pre-trained teacher models are readily available and unlabeled data are abundant. In contrast, data labeling is often laborious and expensive. Finally, we propose a fast feature affinity (FFA) loss that accurately approximates FA loss with a lower order of computational complexity, which helps speed up training for high resolution image input.
summary: In this paper, we propose a feature affinity (FA) assisted knowledge distillation (KD) method to improve quantization-aware training of deep neural networks (DNN). The FA loss on intermediate feature maps of DNNs plays the role of teaching middle steps of a solution to a student instead of only giving final answers in the conventional KD where the loss acts on the network logits at the output level. Combining logit loss and FA loss, we found that the quantized student network receives stronger supervision than from the labeled ground-truth data. The resulting FAQD is capable of compressing model on label-free data, which brings immediate practical benefits as pre-trained teacher models are readily available and unlabeled data are abundant. In contrast, data labeling is often laborious and expensive. Finally, we propose a fast feature affinity (FFA) loss that accurately approximates FA loss with a lower order of computational complexity, which helps speed up training for high resolution image input.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种特征亲和力（FA）辅助知识蒸馏（KD）方法来改进深度神经网络（DNN）的量化感知训练。 DNN 中间特征图上的 FA 损失起到了向学生教授解决方案的中间步骤的作用，而不是仅在传统 KD 中给出最终答案，其中损失作用于输出级别的网络 logits。结合 logit 损失和 FA 损失，我们发现量化的学生网络比标记的真实数据受到更强的监督。由此产生的 FAQD 能够在无标签数据上压缩模型，这带来了直接的实际好处，因为预训练的教师模型很容易获得，并且无标签数据也很丰富。相比之下，数据标记通常既费力又昂贵。最后，我们提出了一种快速特征亲和力（FFA）损失，它以较低的计算复杂度准确地近似 FA 损失，这有助于加快高分辨率图像输入的训练。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于特征亲和力（FA）辅助的知识蒸馏方法，用于在无标签数据上改进深度神经网络的量化训练。
AI.motivation: 传统知识蒸馏依赖标注数据，而标注成本高昂。本文旨在利用丰富的无标签数据进行模型压缩，降低对标注数据的依赖。
AI.method: 结合输出层的logit损失与中间特征的FA损失，通过预训练教师模型指导学生网络训练，并提出计算复杂度更低的快速特征亲和力（FFA）损失以加速训练。
AI.result: FAKD方法在无标签数据上实现有效模型压缩，且FFA损失显著提升高分辨率图像训练效率，无需依赖标注数据即可获得良好性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为实际应用中缺乏标注数据的场景提供了高效且实用的模型压缩方案，具有重要的工程应用价值。

motivation: 传统知识蒸馏依赖标注数据，而标注成本高昂。本文旨在利用丰富的无标签数据进行模型压缩，降低对标注数据的依赖。
method: 结合输出层的logit损失与中间特征的FA损失，通过预训练教师模型指导学生网络训练，并提出计算复杂度更低的快速特征亲和力（FFA）损失以加速训练。
result: FAKD方法在无标签数据上实现有效模型压缩，且FFA损失显著提升高分辨率图像训练效率，无需依赖标注数据即可获得良好性能。
conclusion: 该方法为实际应用中缺乏标注数据的场景提供了高效且实用的模型压缩方案，具有重要的工程应用价值。
search_text: Feature Affinity Assisted Knowledge Distillation and Quantization of Deep Neural Networks on Label-Free Data 无标签数据上深度神经网络的特征亲和力辅助知识蒸馏和量化 In this paper, we propose a feature affinity (FA) assisted knowledge distillation (KD) method to improve quantization-aware training of deep neural networks (DNN). The FA loss on intermediate feature maps of DNNs plays the role of teaching middle steps of a solution to a student instead of only giving final answers in the conventional KD where the loss acts on the network logits at the output level. Combining logit loss and FA loss, we found that the quantized student network receives stronger supervision than from the labeled ground-truth data. The resulting FAQD is capable of compressing model on label-free data, which brings immediate practical benefits as pre-trained teacher models are readily available and unlabeled data are abundant. In contrast, data labeling is often laborious and expensive. Finally, we propose a fast feature affinity (FFA) loss that accurately approximates FA loss with a lower order of computational complexity, which helps speed up training for high resolution image input. 在本文中，我们提出了一种特征亲和力（FA）辅助知识蒸馏（KD）方法来改进深度神经网络（DNN）的量化感知训练。 DNN 中间特征图上的 FA 损失起到了向学生教授解决方案的中间步骤的作用，而不是仅在传统 KD 中给出最终答案，其中损失作用于输出级别的网络 logits。结合 logit 损失和 FA 损失，我们发现量化的学生网络比标记的真实数据受到更强的监督。由此产生的 FAQD 能够在无标签数据上压缩模型，这带来了直接的实际好处，因为预训练的教师模型很容易获得，并且无标签数据也很丰富。相比之下，数据标记通常既费力又昂贵。最后，我们提出了一种快速特征亲和力（FFA）损失，它以较低的计算复杂度准确地近似 FA 损失，这有助于加快高分辨率图像输入的训练。 本文提出一种基于特征亲和力（FA）辅助的知识蒸馏方法，用于在无标签数据上改进深度神经网络的量化训练。 Zhijian Li,Biao Yang,Penghang Yin,Yingyong Qi,Jack Xin cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.IT,math.NA

authors_text: Ryusei Kimura,Takahiro Tanaka,Yuki Yoshihara,Kazuhiro Fujikake,Hitoshi Kanamori,Shogo Okada
title: Estimating Driver Personality Traits from On-Road Driving Data
title_zh: 从道路驾驶数据估计驾驶员性格特征
abstract: This paper focuses on the estimation of a driver's psychological characteristics using driving data for driving assistance systems. Driving assistance systems that support drivers by adapting individual psychological characteristics can provide appropriate feedback and prevent traffic accidents. As a first step toward implementing such adaptive assistance systems, this research aims to develop a model to estimate drivers' psychological characteristics, such as cognitive function, psychological driving style, and workload sensitivity, from on-road driving behavioral data using machine learning and deep learning techniques. We also investigated the relationship between driving behavior and various cognitive functions, including the Trail Making Test (TMT) and Useful Field of View (UFOV) test, through regression modeling. The proposed method focuses on road type information and captures various durations of time-series data observed from driving behaviors. First, we segment the driving time-series data into two road types, namely, arterial roads and intersections, to consider driving situations. Second, we further segment data into many sequences of various durations. Third, statistics are calculated from each sequence. Finally, these statistics are used as input features of machine learning models to estimate psychological characteristics. The experimental results show that our model can estimate a driver's cognitive function, namely, the TMT~(B) and UFOV test scores, with Pearson correlation coefficients $r$ of 0.579 and 0.708, respectively. Some characteristics, such as psychological driving style and workload sensitivity, are estimated with high accuracy, but whether various duration segmentation improves accuracy depends on the characteristics, and it is not effective for all characteristics.
summary: This paper focuses on the estimation of a driver's psychological characteristics using driving data for driving assistance systems. Driving assistance systems that support drivers by adapting individual psychological characteristics can provide appropriate feedback and prevent traffic accidents. As a first step toward implementing such adaptive assistance systems, this research aims to develop a model to estimate drivers' psychological characteristics, such as cognitive function, psychological driving style, and workload sensitivity, from on-road driving behavioral data using machine learning and deep learning techniques. We also investigated the relationship between driving behavior and various cognitive functions, including the Trail Making Test (TMT) and Useful Field of View (UFOV) test, through regression modeling. The proposed method focuses on road type information and captures various durations of time-series data observed from driving behaviors. First, we segment the driving time-series data into two road types, namely, arterial roads and intersections, to consider driving situations. Second, we further segment data into many sequences of various durations. Third, statistics are calculated from each sequence. Finally, these statistics are used as input features of machine learning models to estimate psychological characteristics. The experimental results show that our model can estimate a driver's cognitive function, namely, the TMT~(B) and UFOV test scores, with Pearson correlation coefficients $r$ of 0.579 and 0.708, respectively. Some characteristics, such as psychological driving style and workload sensitivity, are estimated with high accuracy, but whether various duration segmentation improves accuracy depends on the characteristics, and it is not effective for all characteristics.
abstract_zh: 本文重点研究使用驾驶辅助系统的驾驶数据来估计驾驶员的心理特征。通过适应个人心理特征来支持驾驶员的驾驶辅助系统可以提供适当的反馈并防止交通事故。作为实施此类自适应辅助系统的第一步，本研究旨在开发一种模型，利用机器学习和深度学习技术，根据道路驾驶行为数据来估计驾驶员的心理特征，例如认知功能、心理驾驶风格和工作负载敏感性。我们还通过回归模型研究了驾驶行为与各种认知功能之间的关系，包括轨迹制作测试（TMT）和有用视野（UFOV）测试。所提出的方法侧重于道路类型信息，并捕获从驾驶行为观察到的各种持续时间的时间序列数据。首先，我们将驾驶时间序列数据分为两种道路类型，即主干道和交叉路口，以考虑驾驶情况。其次，我们进一步将数据分割成不同持续时间的许多序列。第三，根据每个序列计算统计数据。最后，这些统计数据用作机器学习模型的输入特征来估计心理特征。实验结果表明，我们的模型可以估计驾驶员的认知功能，即TMT~(B)和UFOV测试分数，皮尔逊相关系数$r$分别为0.579和0.708。一些特征，例如心理驾驶风格和工作负载敏感性，估计的准确性很高，但各种持续时间分段是否提高准确性取决于特征，并且并不是对所有特征都有效。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过车载驾驶数据估计驾驶员的心理特征，以开发适应性驾驶辅助系统。
AI.motivation: 通过适应驾驶员心理特征的辅助系统提供个性化反馈，可预防交通事故，因此需建立基于驾驶行为数据的心理特征估计模型。
AI.method: 将驾驶时序数据按道路类型（主干道、交叉口）分割，并进一步划分为多段序列，提取统计特征作为机器学习模型输入，结合回归模型分析驾驶行为与认知功能（如TMT、UFOV测试）的关系。
AI.result: 模型对TMT(B)和UFOV测试得分的皮尔逊相关系数分别为0.579和0.708，心理驾驶风格和工作负载敏感性估计准确率较高，但不同特征对时序分割的敏感性存在差异。
AI.conclusion: 基于道路类型与时序分割的特征提取方法能有效估计部分认知功能，但需针对不同心理特征优化数据处理策略。

authors_text: Yuan Yuan,Huandong Wang,Jingtao Ding,Depeng Jin,Yong Li
title: Learning to Simulate Daily Activities via Modeling Dynamic Human Needs
title_zh: 学习通过模拟人类动态需求来模拟日常活动
abstract: Daily activity data that records individuals' various types of activities in daily life are widely used in many applications such as activity scheduling, activity recommendation, and policymaking. Though with high value, its accessibility is limited due to high collection costs and potential privacy issues. Therefore, simulating human activities to produce massive high-quality data is of great importance to benefit practical applications. However, existing solutions, including rule-based methods with simplified assumptions of human behavior and data-driven methods directly fitting real-world data, both cannot fully qualify for matching reality. In this paper, motivated by the classic psychological theory, Maslow's need theory describing human motivation, we propose a knowledge-driven simulation framework based on generative adversarial imitation learning. To enhance the fidelity and utility of the generated activity data, our core idea is to model the evolution of human needs as the underlying mechanism that drives activity generation in the simulation model. Specifically, this is achieved by a hierarchical model structure that disentangles different need levels, and the use of neural stochastic differential equations that successfully captures piecewise-continuous characteristics of need dynamics. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in terms of data fidelity and utility. Besides, we present the insightful interpretability of the need modeling. The code is available at https://github.com/tsinghua-fib-lab/SAND.
summary: Daily activity data that records individuals' various types of activities in daily life are widely used in many applications such as activity scheduling, activity recommendation, and policymaking. Though with high value, its accessibility is limited due to high collection costs and potential privacy issues. Therefore, simulating human activities to produce massive high-quality data is of great importance to benefit practical applications. However, existing solutions, including rule-based methods with simplified assumptions of human behavior and data-driven methods directly fitting real-world data, both cannot fully qualify for matching reality. In this paper, motivated by the classic psychological theory, Maslow's need theory describing human motivation, we propose a knowledge-driven simulation framework based on generative adversarial imitation learning. To enhance the fidelity and utility of the generated activity data, our core idea is to model the evolution of human needs as the underlying mechanism that drives activity generation in the simulation model. Specifically, this is achieved by a hierarchical model structure that disentangles different need levels, and the use of neural stochastic differential equations that successfully captures piecewise-continuous characteristics of need dynamics. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in terms of data fidelity and utility. Besides, we present the insightful interpretability of the need modeling. The code is available at https://github.com/tsinghua-fib-lab/SAND.
abstract_zh: 记录个人日常生活中各类活动的日常活动数据被广泛应用于活动调度、活动推荐、政策制定等众多应用中。尽管其价值很高，但由于收集成本高和潜在的隐私问题，其可访问性受到限制。因此，模拟人类活动产生海量高质量数据对于实际应用具有重要意义。然而，现有的解决方案，包括简化人类行为假设的基于规则的方法和直接拟合现实世界数据的数据驱动方法，都无法完全符合现实。在本文中，受经典心理学理论——描述人类动机的马斯洛需求理论的启发，我们提出了一种基于生成对抗性模仿学习的知识驱动模拟框架。为了增强生成的活动数据的保真度和实用性，我们的核心思想是将人类需求的演变建模为模拟模型中驱动活动生成的基本机制。具体来说，这是通过分解不同需求级别的分层模型结构以及成功捕获需求动态的分段连续特征的神经随机微分方程的使用来实现的。大量的实验表明，我们的框架在数据保真度和实用性方面优于最先进的基线。此外，我们还提出了需求建模的富有洞察力的可解释性。代码可在 https://github.com/tsinghua-fib-lab/SAND 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于动态需求建模的模拟框架，用于生成高质量的日常活动数据。
AI.motivation: 现有方法（规则驱动和数据驱动）无法准确模拟现实中的复杂人类行为，且存在数据获取成本高和隐私问题。
AI.method: 结合马斯洛需求理论，设计分层模型结构与神经随机微分方程，通过生成对抗模仿学习框架建模需求动态演化。
AI.result: 在数据保真度和实用性上优于现有方法，并提供了需求建模的可解释性分析。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过动态需求建模有效提升了日常活动模拟的真实性，为相关应用提供了高质量数据支持。

Therefore, simulating human activities to produce massive high-quality data is of great importance to benefit practical applications. However, existing solutions, including rule-based methods with simplified assumptions of human behavior and data-driven methods directly fitting real-world data, both cannot fully qualify for matching reality. In this paper, motivated by the classic psychological theory, Maslow's need theory describing human motivation, we propose a knowledge-driven simulation framework based on generative adversarial imitation learning. To enhance the fidelity and utility of the generated activity data, our core idea is to model the evolution of human needs as the underlying mechanism that drives activity generation in the simulation model. Specifically, this is achieved by a hierarchical model structure that disentangles different need levels, and the use of neural stochastic differential equations that successfully captures piecewise-continuous characteristics of need dynamics. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in terms of data fidelity and utility. Besides, we present the insightful interpretability of the need modeling. The code is available at https://github.com/tsinghua-fib-lab/SAND. 记录个人日常生活中各类活动的日常活动数据被广泛应用于活动调度、活动推荐、政策制定等众多应用中。尽管其价值很高，但由于收集成本高和潜在的隐私问题，其可访问性受到限制。因此，模拟人类活动产生海量高质量数据对于实际应用具有重要意义。然而，现有的解决方案，包括简化人类行为假设的基于规则的方法和直接拟合现实世界数据的数据驱动方法，都无法完全符合现实。在本文中，受经典心理学理论——描述人类动机的马斯洛需求理论的启发，我们提出了一种基于生成对抗性模仿学习的知识驱动模拟框架。为了增强生成的活动数据的保真度和实用性，我们的核心思想是将人类需求的演变建模为模拟模型中驱动活动生成的基本机制。具体来说，这是通过分解不同需求级别的分层模型结构以及成功捕获需求动态的分段连续特征的神经随机微分方程的使用来实现的。大量的实验表明，我们的框架在数据保真度和实用性方面优于最先进的基线。此外，我们还提出了需求建模的富有洞察力的可解释性。代码可在 https://github.com/tsinghua-fib-lab/SAND 获取。 本文提出一种基于动态需求建模的模拟框架，用于生成高质量的日常活动数据。 Yuan Yuan,Huandong Wang,Jingtao Ding,Depeng Jin,Yong Li cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Xiaoyun Xu,Guilherme Perin,Stjepan Picek
title: IB-RAR: Information Bottleneck as Regularizer for Adversarial Robustness
title_zh: IB-RAR：信息瓶颈作为对抗鲁棒性的正则化器
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel method, IB-RAR, which uses Information Bottleneck (IB) to strengthen adversarial robustness for both adversarial training and non-adversarial-trained methods. We first use the IB theory to build regularizers as learning objectives in the loss function. Then, we filter out unnecessary features of intermediate representation according to their mutual information (MI) with labels, as the network trained with IB provides easily distinguishable MI for its features. Experimental results show that our method can be naturally combined with adversarial training and provides consistently better accuracy on new adversarial examples. Our method improves the accuracy by an average of 3.07% against five adversarial attacks for the VGG16 network, trained with three adversarial training benchmarks and the CIFAR-10 dataset. In addition, our method also provides good robustness for undefended methods, such as training with cross-entropy loss only. Finally, in the absence of adversarial training, the VGG16 network trained using our method and the CIFAR-10 dataset reaches an accuracy of 35.86% against PGD examples, while using all layers reaches 25.61% accuracy.
summary: In this paper, we propose a novel method, IB-RAR, which uses Information Bottleneck (IB) to strengthen adversarial robustness for both adversarial training and non-adversarial-trained methods. We first use the IB theory to build regularizers as learning objectives in the loss function. Then, we filter out unnecessary features of intermediate representation according to their mutual information (MI) with labels, as the network trained with IB provides easily distinguishable MI for its features. Experimental results show that our method can be naturally combined with adversarial training and provides consistently better accuracy on new adversarial examples. Our method improves the accuracy by an average of 3.07% against five adversarial attacks for the VGG16 network, trained with three adversarial training benchmarks and the CIFAR-10 dataset. In addition, our method also provides good robustness for undefended methods, such as training with cross-entropy loss only. Finally, in the absence of adversarial training, the VGG16 network trained using our method and the CIFAR-10 dataset reaches an accuracy of 35.86% against PGD examples, while using all layers reaches 25.61% accuracy.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种新方法 IB-RAR，它使用信息瓶颈（IB）来增强对抗训练和非对抗训练方法的对抗鲁棒性。我们首先使用 IB 理论构建正则化器作为损失函数中的学习目标。然后，我们根据中间表示的互信息（MI）和标签过滤掉不必要的特征，因为用 IB 训练的网络为其特征提供了易于区分的 MI。实验结果表明，我们的方法可以自然地与对抗训练相结合，并在新的对抗样本上始终提供更好的准确性。我们的方法针对 VGG16 网络的五种对抗性攻击的准确度平均提高了 3.07%，并使用三个对抗性训练基准和 CIFAR-10 数据集进行训练。此外，我们的方法还为无防御方法提供了良好的鲁棒性，例如仅使用交叉熵损失进行训练。最后，在没有对抗性训练的情况下，使用我们的方法和 CIFAR-10 数据集训练的 VGG16 网络相对于 PGD 示例的准确率达到 35.86%，而使用所有层的准确率达到 25.61%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为IB-RAR的新方法，通过信息瓶颈理论来增强对抗鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 对抗训练方法在提高模型鲁棒性方面存在局限，因此需要一种新的方法来增强对抗鲁棒性。
AI.method: 该方法利用信息瓶颈理论构建正则化项，并根据中间表示与标签的互信息过滤掉不必要的特征。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在对抗训练和非对抗训练方法中均能提高准确率，平均提升3.07%。
AI.conclusion: IB-RAR方法在对抗鲁棒性方面表现出色，适用于多种训练方法，并在无对抗训练情况下仍能保持较高的准确率。

authors_text: Bas Peters
title: CQnet: convex-geometric interpretation and constraining neural-network trajectories
title_zh: CQnet：凸几何解释和约束神经网络轨迹
abstract: We introduce CQnet, a neural network with origins in the CQ algorithm for solving convex split-feasibility problems and forward-backward splitting. CQnet's trajectories are interpretable as particles that are tracking a changing constraint set via its point-to-set distance function while being elements of another constraint set at every layer. More than just a convex-geometric interpretation, CQnet accommodates learned and deterministic constraints that may be sample or data-specific and are satisfied by every layer and the output. Furthermore, the states in CQnet progress toward another constraint set at every layer. We provide proof of stability/nonexpansiveness with minimal assumptions. The combination of constraint handling and stability put forward CQnet as a candidate for various tasks where prior knowledge exists on the network states or output.
summary: We introduce CQnet, a neural network with origins in the CQ algorithm for solving convex split-feasibility problems and forward-backward splitting. CQnet's trajectories are interpretable as particles that are tracking a changing constraint set via its point-to-set distance function while being elements of another constraint set at every layer. More than just a convex-geometric interpretation, CQnet accommodates learned and deterministic constraints that may be sample or data-specific and are satisfied by every layer and the output. Furthermore, the states in CQnet progress toward another constraint set at every layer. We provide proof of stability/nonexpansiveness with minimal assumptions. The combination of constraint handling and stability put forward CQnet as a candidate for various tasks where prior knowledge exists on the network states or output.
abstract_zh: 我们介绍 CQnet，这是一种起源于 CQ 算法的神经网络，用于解决凸分裂可行性问题和前向-后向分裂。 CQnet 的轨迹可以解释为粒子，这些粒子通过其点到集距离函数跟踪不断变化的约束集，同时又是每一层另一个约束集的元素。 CQnet 不仅仅是凸几何解释，它还适应学习的和确定性的约束，这些约束可能是特定于样本或数据的，并且每一层和输出都满足这些约束。此外，CQnet 中的状态在每一层都朝着另一个约束集发展。我们以最小的假设提供稳定性/非扩张性的证明。约束处理和稳定性的结合使 CQnet 成为网络状态或输出存在先验知识的各种任务的候选者。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CQnet神经网络，通过凸几何解释和约束处理，实现可解释且稳定的神经网络轨迹控制。
AI.motivation: 传统神经网络缺乏对约束的显式处理和可解释性，而实际任务中常存在先验约束知识需被满足。
AI.method: 基于CQ算法框架，将网络轨迹解释为粒子在约束集间演化，结合学习到的约束条件并证明其稳定性。
AI.result: 证明CQnet具有稳定性与非扩张性，约束条件在各层均被满足，适用于需先验约束的任务场景。
AI.conclusion: CQnet通过凸几何视角和约束机制，为需要可解释性与稳定性的神经网络任务提供了新方法。

authors_text: Stephen Casper,Yuxiao Li,Jiawei Li,Tong Bu,Kevin Zhang,Kaivalya Hariharan,Dylan Hadfield-Menell
title: Red Teaming Deep Neural Networks with Feature Synthesis Tools
title_zh: 红队深度神经网络与特征合成工具
comment: In Proceedings of the 37th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2023)
comments: In Proceedings of the 37th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2023)
abstract: Interpretable AI tools are often motivated by the goal of understanding model behavior in out-of-distribution (OOD) contexts. Despite the attention this area of study receives, there are comparatively few cases where these tools have identified previously unknown bugs in models. We argue that this is due, in part, to a common feature of many interpretability methods: they analyze model behavior by using a particular dataset. This only allows for the study of the model in the context of features that the user can sample in advance. To address this, a growing body of research involves interpreting models using \emph{feature synthesis} methods that do not depend on a dataset. In this paper, we benchmark the usefulness of interpretability tools on debugging tasks. Our key insight is that we can implant human-interpretable trojans into models and then evaluate these tools based on whether they can help humans discover them. This is analogous to finding OOD bugs, except the ground truth is known, allowing us to know when an interpretation is correct. We make four contributions. (1) We propose trojan discovery as an evaluation task for interpretability tools and introduce a benchmark with 12 trojans of 3 different types. (2) We demonstrate the difficulty of this benchmark with a preliminary evaluation of 16 state-of-the-art feature attribution/saliency tools. Even under ideal conditions, given direct access to data with the trojan trigger, these methods still often fail to identify bugs. (3) We evaluate 7 feature-synthesis methods on our benchmark. (4) We introduce and evaluate 2 new variants of the best-performing method from the previous evaluation. A website for this paper and its code is at https://benchmarking-interpretability.csail.mit.edu/
summary: Interpretable AI tools are often motivated by the goal of understanding model behavior in out-of-distribution (OOD) contexts. Despite the attention this area of study receives, there are comparatively few cases where these tools have identified previously unknown bugs in models. We argue that this is due, in part, to a common feature of many interpretability methods: they analyze model behavior by using a particular dataset. This only allows for the study of the model in the context of features that the user can sample in advance. To address this, a growing body of research involves interpreting models using \emph{feature synthesis} methods that do not depend on a dataset. In this paper, we benchmark the usefulness of interpretability tools on debugging tasks. Our key insight is that we can implant human-interpretable trojans into models and then evaluate these tools based on whether they can help humans discover them. This is analogous to finding OOD bugs, except the ground truth is known, allowing us to know when an interpretation is correct. We make four contributions. (1) We propose trojan discovery as an evaluation task for interpretability tools and introduce a benchmark with 12 trojans of 3 different types. (2) We demonstrate the difficulty of this benchmark with a preliminary evaluation of 16 state-of-the-art feature attribution/saliency tools. Even under ideal conditions, given direct access to data with the trojan trigger, these methods still often fail to identify bugs. (3) We evaluate 7 feature-synthesis methods on our benchmark. (4) We introduce and evaluate 2 new variants of the best-performing method from the previous evaluation. A website for this paper and its code is at https://benchmarking-interpretability.csail.mit.edu/
abstract_zh: 可解释的人工智能工具通常是出于理解分布外（OOD）环境中模型行为的目标而开发的。尽管这一研究领域受到关注，但这些工具识别模型中以前未知的错误的情况相对较少。我们认为，这在一定程度上是由于许多可解释性方法的共同特征：它们通过使用特定的数据集来分析模型行为。这只允许在用户可以提前采样的特征的背景下研究模型。为了解决这个问题，越来越多的研究涉及使用不依赖于数据集的 \emph{特征合成} 方法来解释模型。在本文中，我们对可解释性工具在调试任务中的有用性进行了基准测试。我们的主要见解是，我们可以将人类可解释的木马植入模型中，然后根据它们是否可以帮助人类发现它们来评估这些工具。这类似于查找 OOD bug，只不过基本事实是已知的，使我们能够知道解释何时是正确的。我们做出四项贡献。 (1) 我们提出将木马发现作为可解释性工具的评估任务，并引入 3 种不同类型的 12 个木马的基准。 (2) 我们通过对 16 个最先进的特征归因/显着性工具的初步评估来证明该基准的难度。即使在理想条件下，如果使用木马触发器直接访问数据，这些方法仍然常常无法识别错误。 (3) 我们在基准测试中评估了 7 种特征合成方法。 (4) 我们引入并评估了先前评估中表现最佳的方法的 2 个新变体。本文及其代码的网站位于 https://benchmarking-interpretability.csail.mit.edu/

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过植入可解释的特洛伊木马作为基准任务，评估特征合成工具在调试深度神经网络中的有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有可解释AI工具依赖特定数据集分析模型行为，难以发现未知的分布外错误，需借助不依赖数据集的特征合成方法进行验证。
AI.method: 构建包含12种特洛伊的基准测试集，评估16种特征归因工具和7种特征合成方法，并提出两种改进方法。
AI.result: 初步实验显示，即使在理想条件下，现有工具仍常无法识别特洛伊；特征合成方法表现更优，新方法进一步提升了检测效果。
AI.conclusion: 当前解释工具在调试任务中效果有限，特征合成方法更有效，未来需开发更强大的工具以发现隐藏的模型缺陷。

search_text: Red Teaming Deep Neural Networks with Feature Synthesis Tools 红队深度神经网络与特征合成工具 Interpretable AI tools are often motivated by the goal of understanding model behavior in out-of-distribution (OOD) contexts. Despite the attention this area of study receives, there are comparatively few cases where these tools have identified previously unknown bugs in models. We argue that this is due, in part, to a common feature of many interpretability methods: they analyze model behavior by using a particular dataset. This only allows for the study of the model in the context of features that the user can sample in advance. To address this, a growing body of research involves interpreting models using \emph{feature synthesis} methods that do not depend on a dataset. In this paper, we benchmark the usefulness of interpretability tools on debugging tasks. Our key insight is that we can implant human-interpretable trojans into models and then evaluate these tools based on whether they can help humans discover them. This is analogous to finding OOD bugs, except the ground truth is known, allowing us to know when an interpretation is correct. We make four contributions. (1) We propose trojan discovery as an evaluation task for interpretability tools and introduce a benchmark with 12 trojans of 3 different types. (2) We demonstrate the difficulty of this benchmark with a preliminary evaluation of 16 state-of-the-art feature attribution/saliency tools. Even under ideal conditions, given direct access to data with the trojan trigger, these methods still often fail to identify bugs. (3) We evaluate 7 feature-synthesis methods on our benchmark. (4) We introduce and evaluate 2 new variants of the best-performing method from the previous evaluation. A website for this paper and its code is at https://benchmarking-interpretability.csail.mit.edu/ 可解释的人工智能工具通常是出于理解分布外（OOD）环境中模型行为的目标而开发的。尽管这一研究领域受到关注，但这些工具识别模型中以前未知的错误的情况相对较少。我们认为，这在一定程度上是由于许多可解释性方法的共同特征：它们通过使用特定的数据集来分析模型行为。这只允许在用户可以提前采样的特征的背景下研究模型。为了解决这个问题，越来越多的研究涉及使用不依赖于数据集的 \emph{特征合成} 方法来解释模型。在本文中，我们对可解释性工具在调试任务中的有用性进行了基准测试。我们的主要见解是，我们可以将人类可解释的木马植入模型中，然后根据它们是否可以帮助人类发现它们来评估这些工具。这类似于查找 OOD bug，只不过基本事实是已知的，使我们能够知道解释何时是正确的。我们做出四项贡献。 (1) 我们提出将木马发现作为可解释性工具的评估任务，并引入 3 种不同类型的 12 个木马的基准。 (2) 我们通过对 16 个最先进的特征归因/显着性工具的初步评估来证明该基准的难度。即使在理想条件下，如果使用木马触发器直接访问数据，这些方法仍然常常无法识别错误。 (3) 我们在基准测试中评估了 7 种特征合成方法。 (4) 我们引入并评估了先前评估中表现最佳的方法的 2 个新变体。本文及其代码的网站位于 https://benchmarking-interpretability.csail.mit.edu/ 本文提出通过植入可解释的特洛伊木马作为基准任务，评估特征合成工具在调试深度神经网络中的有效性。 Stephen Casper,Yuxiao Li,Jiawei Li,Tong Bu,Kevin Zhang,Kaivalya Hariharan,Dylan Hadfield-Menell cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Felix Friedrich,Manuel Brack,Lukas Struppek,Dominik Hintersdorf,Patrick Schramowski,Sasha Luccioni,Kristian Kersting
title: Fair Diffusion: Instructing Text-to-Image Generation Models on Fairness
title_zh: 公平扩散：指导文本到图像生成模型的公平性
abstract: Generative AI models have recently achieved astonishing results in quality and are consequently employed in a fast-growing number of applications. However, since they are highly data-driven, relying on billion-sized datasets randomly scraped from the internet, they also suffer from degenerated and biased human behavior, as we demonstrate. In fact, they may even reinforce such biases. To not only uncover but also combat these undesired effects, we present a novel strategy, called Fair Diffusion, to attenuate biases after the deployment of generative text-to-image models. Specifically, we demonstrate shifting a bias, based on human instructions, in any direction yielding arbitrarily new proportions for, e.g., identity groups. As our empirical evaluation demonstrates, this introduced control enables instructing generative image models on fairness, with no data filtering and additional training required.
summary: Generative AI models have recently achieved astonishing results in quality and are consequently employed in a fast-growing number of applications. However, since they are highly data-driven, relying on billion-sized datasets randomly scraped from the internet, they also suffer from degenerated and biased human behavior, as we demonstrate. In fact, they may even reinforce such biases. To not only uncover but also combat these undesired effects, we present a novel strategy, called Fair Diffusion, to attenuate biases after the deployment of generative text-to-image models. Specifically, we demonstrate shifting a bias, based on human instructions, in any direction yielding arbitrarily new proportions for, e.g., identity groups. As our empirical evaluation demonstrates, this introduced control enables instructing generative image models on fairness, with no data filtering and additional training required.
abstract_zh: 生成式人工智能模型最近在质量方面取得了惊人的成果，因此被用于越来越多的应用程序中。然而，由于它们是高度数据驱动的，依赖于从互联网上随机抓取的数十亿规模的数据集，因此它们也遭受退化和偏见的人类行为的影响，正如我们所证明的那样。事实上，它们甚至可能强化这种偏见。为了不仅发现而且对抗这些不良影响，我们提出了一种称为“公平扩散”的新颖策略，以在部署生成文本到图像模型后减少偏见。具体来说，我们展示了根据人类指令向任何方向转移偏见，从而产生任意新的比例，例如身份群体。正如我们的实证评估所表明的那样，这种引入的控制能够指导生成图像模型的公平性，而无需数据过滤和额外的训练。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Fair Diffusion的方法，在无需额外训练或数据过滤的情况下，通过人类指令调整文本到图像生成模型的偏见，以提升生成结果的公平性。
AI.motivation: 生成式AI模型因依赖大规模互联网数据而继承并可能强化数据中的偏见，现有方法难以有效解决这一问题。
AI.method: 通过后部署指令控制，基于人类输入动态调整生成模型输出中身份群体比例，直接干预生成过程以减弱偏见。
AI.result: 实证表明该方法能有效指导生成模型实现公平性目标，且无需数据过滤或额外训练。
AI.conclusion: Fair Diffusion为在保持模型性能的同时解决AI生成结果中的偏见问题提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Tobias Thummerer,Lars Mikelsons
title: Eigen-informed NeuralODEs: Dealing with stability and convergence issues of NeuralODEs
title_zh: 基于特征的 NeuralODE：处理 NeuralODE 的稳定性和收敛问题
abstract: Using vanilla NeuralODEs to model large and/or complex systems often fails due two reasons: Stability and convergence. NeuralODEs are capable of describing stable as well as instable dynamic systems. Selecting an appropriate numerical solver is not trivial, because NeuralODE properties change during training. If the NeuralODE becomes more stiff, a suboptimal solver may need to perform very small solver steps, which significantly slows down the training process. If the NeuralODE becomes to instable, the numerical solver might not be able to solve it at all, which causes the training process to terminate. Often, this is tackled by choosing a computational expensive solver that is robust to instable and stiff ODEs, but at the cost of a significantly decreased training performance. Our method on the other hand, allows to enforce ODE properties that fit a specific solver or application-related boundary conditions. Concerning the convergence behavior, NeuralODEs often tend to run into local minima, especially if the system to be learned is highly dynamic and/or oscillating over multiple periods. Because of the vanishing gradient at a local minimum, the NeuralODE is often not capable of leaving it and converge to the right solution. We present a technique to add knowledge of ODE properties based on eigenvalues - like (partly) stability, oscillation capability, frequency, damping and/or stiffness - to the training objective of a NeuralODE. We exemplify our method at a linear as well as a nonlinear system model and show, that the presented training process is far more robust against local minima, instabilities and sparse data samples and improves training convergence and performance.
summary: Using vanilla NeuralODEs to model large and/or complex systems often fails due two reasons: Stability and convergence. NeuralODEs are capable of describing stable as well as instable dynamic systems. Selecting an appropriate numerical solver is not trivial, because NeuralODE properties change during training. If the NeuralODE becomes more stiff, a suboptimal solver may need to perform very small solver steps, which significantly slows down the training process. If the NeuralODE becomes to instable, the numerical solver might not be able to solve it at all, which causes the training process to terminate. Often, this is tackled by choosing a computational expensive solver that is robust to instable and stiff ODEs, but at the cost of a significantly decreased training performance. Our method on the other hand, allows to enforce ODE properties that fit a specific solver or application-related boundary conditions. Concerning the convergence behavior, NeuralODEs often tend to run into local minima, especially if the system to be learned is highly dynamic and/or oscillating over multiple periods. Because of the vanishing gradient at a local minimum, the NeuralODE is often not capable of leaving it and converge to the right solution. We present a technique to add knowledge of ODE properties based on eigenvalues - like (partly) stability, oscillation capability, frequency, damping and/or stiffness - to the training objective of a NeuralODE. We exemplify our method at a linear as well as a nonlinear system model and show, that the presented training process is far more robust against local minima, instabilities and sparse data samples and improves training convergence and performance.
abstract_zh: 使用普通 NeuralODE 对大型和/或复杂系统进行建模通常会失败，原因有两个：稳定性和收敛性。 NeuralODE 能够描述稳定和不稳定的动态系统。选择合适的数值求解器并非易事，因为 NeuralODE 属性在训练期间会发生变化。如果 NeuralODE 变得更加僵硬，次优求解器可能需要执行非常小的求解器步骤，这会显着减慢训练过程。如果 NeuralODE 变得不稳定，数值求解器可能根本无法求解，从而导致训练过程终止。通常，可以通过选择计算量大的求解器来解决这个问题，该求解器对于不稳定和刚性的 ODE 具有鲁棒性，但代价是训练性能显着下降。另一方面，我们的方法允许强制执行适合特定求解器或应用程序相关边界条件的 ODE 属性。关于收敛行为，NeuralODE 通常会遇到局部最小值，特别是当要学习的系统高度动态和/或在多个周期内振荡时。由于局部极小值处的梯度消失，NeuralODE 通常无法离开它并收敛到正确的解决方案。我们提出了一种技术，将基于特征值（例如（部分）稳定性、振荡能力、频率、阻尼和/或刚度）的 ODE 属性知识添加到 NeuralODE 的训练目标中。我们在线性和非线性系统模型中举例说明了我们的方法，并表明，所提出的训练过程对于局部极小值、不稳定性和稀疏数据样本更加稳健，并提高了训练收敛性和性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于特征值信息的神经常微分方程（NeuralODE）方法，以解决其稳定性与收敛性问题。
AI.motivation: 传统NeuralODE在建模复杂系统时易受稳定性与收敛性影响，数值求解器选择困难且易陷入局部最优解。
AI.method: 通过将ODE的特征值属性（如稳定性、振荡性、阻尼等）融入训练目标函数，引导模型满足特定求解器或应用需求。
AI.result: 该方法在稀疏数据和动态系统中表现出更强的鲁棒性，有效提升了训练收敛速度与模型性能。
AI.conclusion: 基于特征值的NeuralODE训练策略能有效缓解稳定性与收敛性问题，为复杂系统建模提供新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于特征值信息的神经常微分方程（NeuralODE）方法，以解决其稳定性与收敛性问题。
