For product distributions and elliptical distributions with known scatter (covariance) matrix, we show that given an $\varepsilon$-corrupted sample, we can with probability at least $1-δ$ estimate its location up to error $O(\varepsilon \sqrt{\log(1/\varepsilon)})$ using $\tfrac{d\log(d) + \log(1/δ)}{\varepsilon^2 \log(1/\varepsilon)}$ samples. This result matches the best-known guarantees for the Gaussian distribution and known SQ lower bounds (up to the $\log(d)$ factor). For elliptical distributions with unknown scatter (covariance) matrix, we propose a sequence of efficient algorithms that approaches this optimal error. Specifically, for every $k \in \mathbb{N}$, we design an estimator using time and samples $\tilde{O}({d^k})$ achieving error $O(\varepsilon^{1-\frac{1}{2k}})$. This matches the error and running time guarantees when assuming certifiably bounded moments of order up to $k$. For unknown covariance, such error bounds of $o(\sqrt{\varepsilon})$ are not even known for (general) sub-Gaussian distributions. Our algorithms are based on a generalization of the well-known filtering technique. We show how this machinery can be combined with Huber-loss-based techniques to work with projections of the noise that behave more nicely than the initial noise. Moreover, we show how SoS proofs can be used to obtain algorithmic guarantees even for distributions without a first moment. We believe that this approach may find other applications in future works. 我们研究在没有矩假设的情况下鲁棒地估计平均值或位置参数的问题。我们表明，对于一大类对称分布，可以有效地实现与高斯设置中相同的误差。我们研究的分布包括任意对称一维分布的乘积，例如乘积柯西分布，以及椭圆分布。对于具有已知散点（协方差）矩阵的乘积分布和椭圆分布，我们表明，给定 $\varepsilon$ 损坏的样本，我们可以使用 $\tfrac{d\log(d) + \log(1/δ)}{\varepsilon^2 以至少 $1-δ$ 的概率估计其位置，最大误差为 $O(\varepsilon \sqrt{\log(1/\varepsilon)})$ \log(1/\varepsilon)}$ 样本。此结果与高斯分布和已知 SQ 下界（最高 $\log(d)$ 因子）的最著名保证相匹配。对于具有未知散点（协方差）矩阵的椭圆分布，我们提出了一系列接近此最佳误差的有效算法。具体来说，对于 \mathbb{N}$ 中的每个 $k，我们使用时间和样本 $\tilde{O}({d^k})$ 设计一个估计器，实现误差 $O(\varepsilon^{1-\frac{1}{2k}})$。当假设阶数可证明有界矩高达 $k$ 时，这与误差和运行时间保证相匹配。对于未知的协方差，$o(\sqrt{\varepsilon})$ 的这种误差范围对于（一般）亚高斯分布来说甚至是未知的。我们的算法基于众所周知的过滤技术的推广。我们展示了如何将这种机制与基于 Huber 损失的技术相结合，以处理比初始噪声表现更好的噪声投影。此外，我们还展示了如何使用 SoS 证明来获得算法保证，即使对于没有初始时刻的分布也是如此。我们相信这种方法可能会在未来的工作中找到其他应用。 本文研究在无需矩条件假设下，对对称分布进行鲁棒均值估计的问题，并提出了高效算法。 Gleb Novikov,David Steurer,Stefan Tiegel cs.DS,cs.LG,stat.ML

authors_text: Yi Liu
title: DSL-Assembly: A Robust and Safe Assembly Strategy
title_zh: DSL-Assembly：稳健且安全的组装策略
abstract: A reinforcement learning (RL) based method that enables the robot to accomplish the assembly-type task with safety regulations is proposed. The overall strategy consists of grasping and assembly, and this paper mainly considers the assembly strategy. Force feedback is used instead of visual feedback to perceive the shape and direction of the hole in this paper. Furthermore, since the emergency stop is triggered when the force output is too large, a force-based dynamic safety lock (DSL) is proposed to limit the pressing force of the robot. Finally, we train and test the robot model with a simulator and build ablation experiments to illustrate the effectiveness of our method. The models are independently tested 500 times in the simulator, and we get an 88.57% success rate with a 4mm gap. These models are transferred to the real world and deployed on a real robot. We conducted independent tests and obtained a 79.63% success rate with a 4mm gap. Simulation environments: https://github.com/0707yiliu/peg-in-hole-with-RL.
summary: A reinforcement learning (RL) based method that enables the robot to accomplish the assembly-type task with safety regulations is proposed. The overall strategy consists of grasping and assembly, and this paper mainly considers the assembly strategy. Force feedback is used instead of visual feedback to perceive the shape and direction of the hole in this paper. Furthermore, since the emergency stop is triggered when the force output is too large, a force-based dynamic safety lock (DSL) is proposed to limit the pressing force of the robot. Finally, we train and test the robot model with a simulator and build ablation experiments to illustrate the effectiveness of our method. The models are independently tested 500 times in the simulator, and we get an 88.57% success rate with a 4mm gap. These models are transferred to the real world and deployed on a real robot. We conducted independent tests and obtained a 79.63% success rate with a 4mm gap. Simulation environments: https://github.com/0707yiliu/peg-in-hole-with-RL.
abstract_zh: 提出了一种基于强化学习（RL）的方法，使机器人能够完成符合安全规定的装配型任务。总体策略包括抓取和组装，本文主要考虑组装策略。本文采用力反馈代替视觉反馈来感知孔的形状和方向。此外，由于当力输出过大时会触发紧急停止，因此提出了基于力的动态安全锁（DSL）来限制机器人的压力。最后，我们用模拟器训练和测试机器人模型，并建立消融实验来说明我们方法的有效性。这些模型在模拟器中独立测试了 500 次，在 4mm 间隙下，我们获得了 88.57% 的成功率。这些模型被转移到现实世界并部署在真实的机器人上。我们进行了独立测试，在间隙为 4mm 的情况下获得了 79.63% 的成功率。模拟环境：https://github.com/0707yiliu/peg-in-hole-with-RL。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于强化学习的机器人装配策略，通过力反馈感知孔位并结合动态安全锁（DSL）确保装配过程安全，最终在模拟和现实环境中均取得较高成功率。
AI.motivation: 传统视觉反馈在装配任务中易受环境干扰，且缺乏对力的精确控制，可能导致零件损坏或机器人故障。需开发更安全、鲁棒的装配方法以满足工业需求。
AI.method: 采用强化学习训练机器人装配策略，利用力反馈替代视觉反馈感知孔位形状与方向；设计力反馈驱动的动态安全锁（DSL）限制最大按压力；通过模拟器训练并进行消融实验验证方法有效性，最后迁移至真实机器人测试。
AI.result: 模拟环境中4mm间隙任务成功率88.57%，真实机器人测试中成功率79.63%，验证了方法的有效性和模拟到现实的迁移能力。
AI.conclusion: DSL-Assembly策略通过力反馈与动态安全锁机制显著提升了装配任务的安全性与成功率，证明了强化学习在复杂装配场景中的可行性，未来可进一步优化力反馈精度或扩展至更多装配任务。

authors_text: Sanat Ramesh,Diego Dall'Alba,Cristians Gonzalez,Tong Yu,Pietro Mascagni,Didier Mutter,Jacques Marescaux,Paolo Fiorini,Nicolas Padoy
title: Weakly Supervised Temporal Convolutional Networks for Fine-grained Surgical Activity Recognition
title_zh: 用于细粒度手术活动识别的弱监督时间卷积网络
abstract: Automatic recognition of fine-grained surgical activities, called steps, is a challenging but crucial task for intelligent intra-operative computer assistance. The development of current vision-based activity recognition methods relies heavily on a high volume of manually annotated data. This data is difficult and time-consuming to generate and requires domain-specific knowledge. In this work, we propose to use coarser and easier-to-annotate activity labels, namely phases, as weak supervision to learn step recognition with fewer step annotated videos. We introduce a step-phase dependency loss to exploit the weak supervision signal. We then employ a Single-Stage Temporal Convolutional Network (SS-TCN) with a ResNet-50 backbone, trained in an end-to-end fashion from weakly annotated videos, for temporal activity segmentation and recognition. We extensively evaluate and show the effectiveness of the proposed method on a large video dataset consisting of 40 laparoscopic gastric bypass procedures and the public benchmark CATARACTS containing 50 cataract surgeries.
summary: Automatic recognition of fine-grained surgical activities, called steps, is a challenging but crucial task for intelligent intra-operative computer assistance. The development of current vision-based activity recognition methods relies heavily on a high volume of manually annotated data. This data is difficult and time-consuming to generate and requires domain-specific knowledge. In this work, we propose to use coarser and easier-to-annotate activity labels, namely phases, as weak supervision to learn step recognition with fewer step annotated videos. We introduce a step-phase dependency loss to exploit the weak supervision signal. We then employ a Single-Stage Temporal Convolutional Network (SS-TCN) with a ResNet-50 backbone, trained in an end-to-end fashion from weakly annotated videos, for temporal activity segmentation and recognition. We extensively evaluate and show the effectiveness of the proposed method on a large video dataset consisting of 40 laparoscopic gastric bypass procedures and the public benchmark CATARACTS containing 50 cataract surgeries.
abstract_zh: 自动识别细粒度的手术活动（称为步骤）对于智能术中计算机辅助来说是一项具有挑战性但至关重要的任务。当前基于视觉的活动识别方法的发展在很大程度上依赖于大量的手动注释数据。生成这些数据既困难又耗时，并且需要特定领域的知识。在这项工作中，我们建议使用更粗糙且更容易注释的活动标签（即阶段）作为弱监督，以更少的步骤注释视频来学习步骤识别。我们引入了阶跃相位依赖性损失来利用弱监督信号。然后，我们采用具有 ResNet-50 主干的单级时间卷积网络 (SS-TCN)，通过弱注释视频以端到端方式进行训练，用于时间活动分割和识别。我们在由 40 个腹腔镜胃绕道手术和包含 50 个白内障手术的公共基准 CATARACTS 组成的大型视频数据集上广泛评估并展示了所提出方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种弱监督时间卷积网络，用于细粒度手术步骤识别。
AI.motivation: 现有手术活动识别方法依赖大量人工标注数据，但标注过程耗时且需专业知识，因此需探索弱监督方法减少标注需求。
AI.method: 引入步骤-阶段依赖损失，利用粗粒度阶段标签作为弱监督信号，结合单阶段时间卷积网络（SS-TCN）与ResNet-50主干网络进行端到端训练。
AI.result: 在包含40例腹腔镜胃旁路手术的内部数据集及CATARACTS基准数据集（50例白内障手术）上验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过弱监督信号实现细粒度手术步骤识别，在减少标注成本的同时保持较高识别性能。

We extensively evaluate and show the effectiveness of the proposed method on a large video dataset consisting of 40 laparoscopic gastric bypass procedures and the public benchmark CATARACTS containing 50 cataract surgeries. 自动识别细粒度的手术活动（称为步骤）对于智能术中计算机辅助来说是一项具有挑战性但至关重要的任务。当前基于视觉的活动识别方法的发展在很大程度上依赖于大量的手动注释数据。生成这些数据既困难又耗时，并且需要特定领域的知识。在这项工作中，我们建议使用更粗糙且更容易注释的活动标签（即阶段）作为弱监督，以更少的步骤注释视频来学习步骤识别。我们引入了阶跃相位依赖性损失来利用弱监督信号。然后，我们采用具有 ResNet-50 主干的单级时间卷积网络 (SS-TCN)，通过弱注释视频以端到端方式进行训练，用于时间活动分割和识别。我们在由 40 个腹腔镜胃绕道手术和包含 50 个白内障手术的公共基准 CATARACTS 组成的大型视频数据集上广泛评估并展示了所提出方法的有效性。 本文提出一种弱监督时间卷积网络，用于细粒度手术步骤识别。 Sanat Ramesh,Diego Dall'Alba,Cristians Gonzalez,Tong Yu,Pietro Mascagni,Didier Mutter,Jacques Marescaux,Paolo Fiorini,Nicolas Padoy cs.CV

authors_text: Yuan Sun,Qiurong Song,Xinning Gui,Fenglong Ma,Ting Wang
title: AutoML in The Wild: Obstacles, Workarounds, and Expectations
title_zh: AutoML 实际应用：障碍、解决方法和期望
comment: In Proceedings of the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI'23), April 23-28, 2023, Hamburg, Germany
comments: In Proceedings of the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI'23), April 23-28, 2023, Hamburg, Germany
abstract: Automated machine learning (AutoML) is envisioned to make ML techniques accessible to ordinary users. Recent work has investigated the role of humans in enhancing AutoML functionality throughout a standard ML workflow. However, it is also critical to understand how users adopt existing AutoML solutions in complex, real-world settings from a holistic perspective. To fill this gap, this study conducted semi-structured interviews of AutoML users (N=19) focusing on understanding (1) the limitations of AutoML encountered by users in their real-world practices, (2) the strategies users adopt to cope with such limitations, and (3) how the limitations and workarounds impact their use of AutoML. Our findings reveal that users actively exercise user agency to overcome three major challenges arising from customizability, transparency, and privacy. Furthermore, users make cautious decisions about whether and how to apply AutoML on a case-by-case basis. Finally, we derive design implications for developing future AutoML solutions.
summary: Automated machine learning (AutoML) is envisioned to make ML techniques accessible to ordinary users. Recent work has investigated the role of humans in enhancing AutoML functionality throughout a standard ML workflow. However, it is also critical to understand how users adopt existing AutoML solutions in complex, real-world settings from a holistic perspective. To fill this gap, this study conducted semi-structured interviews of AutoML users (N=19) focusing on understanding (1) the limitations of AutoML encountered by users in their real-world practices, (2) the strategies users adopt to cope with such limitations, and (3) how the limitations and workarounds impact their use of AutoML. Our findings reveal that users actively exercise user agency to overcome three major challenges arising from customizability, transparency, and privacy. Furthermore, users make cautious decisions about whether and how to apply AutoML on a case-by-case basis. Finally, we derive design implications for developing future AutoML solutions.
abstract_zh: 自动化机器学习 (AutoML) 旨在让普通用户能够使用 ML 技术。最近的工作研究了人类在整个标准 ML 工作流程中增强 AutoML 功能的作用。然而，从整体角度了解用户如何在复杂的现实环境中采用现有的 AutoML 解决方案也至关重要。为了填补这一空白，本研究对 AutoML 用户 (N=19) 进行了半结构化访谈，重点是了解 (1) 用户在现实实践中遇到的 AutoML 的局限性，(2) 用户为应对这些限制而采取的策略，以及 (3) 这些限制和解决方法如何影响他们对 AutoML 的使用。我们的研究结果表明，用户积极行使用户代理权来克服可定制性、透明度和隐私带来的三大挑战。此外，用户会根据具体情况对是否以及如何应用 AutoML 做出谨慎的决定。最后，我们得出了开发未来 AutoML 解决方案的设计含义。

AI.tldr: 本文通过用户访谈研究AutoML在真实场景中的应用障碍、应对策略及用户期望，为未来AutoML系统设计提供启示。
AI.motivation: 尽管AutoML旨在降低机器学习门槛，但现有研究缺乏对用户在复杂现实环境中实际使用AutoML时面临挑战的系统性理解。
AI.method: 对19名AutoML用户进行半结构化访谈，聚焦三个核心问题：实际使用中的限制、应对策略及这些因素对AutoML使用的影响。
AI.result: 用户通过主动策略应对定制性、透明性和隐私性三大挑战，且在应用AutoML时采取审慎的案例分析决策方式。
AI.conclusion: 未来AutoML设计需增强用户自主性，平衡系统自动化与用户控制需求，同时提升算法透明度和隐私保护机制。

authors_text: Roberto Bargetto,Federico Della Croce,Rosario Scatamacchia
title: ITERATED INSIDE OUT: a new exact algorithm for the transportation problem
title_zh: 由内而外迭代：解决运输问题的新精确算法
abstract: We propose a novel exact algorithm for the transportation problem, one of the paradigmatic network optimization problems. The algorithm, denoted Iterated Inside Out, requires in input a basic feasible solution and is composed by two main phases that are iteratively repeated until an optimal basic feasible solution is reached. In the first "inside" phase, the algorithm progressively improves upon a given basic solution by increasing the value of several non-basic variables with negative reduced cost. This phase typically outputs a non-basic feasible solution interior to the constraints set polytope. The second "out" phase moves in the opposite direction by iteratively setting to zero several variables until a new improved basic feasible solution is reached. Extensive computational tests show that the proposed approach strongly outperforms all versions of network and linear programming algorithms available in the commercial solvers Cplex and Gurobi and other exact algorithms available in the literature.
summary: We propose a novel exact algorithm for the transportation problem, one of the paradigmatic network optimization problems. The algorithm, denoted Iterated Inside Out, requires in input a basic feasible solution and is composed by two main phases that are iteratively repeated until an optimal basic feasible solution is reached. In the first "inside" phase, the algorithm progressively improves upon a given basic solution by increasing the value of several non-basic variables with negative reduced cost. This phase typically outputs a non-basic feasible solution interior to the constraints set polytope. The second "out" phase moves in the opposite direction by iteratively setting to zero several variables until a new improved basic feasible solution is reached. Extensive computational tests show that the proposed approach strongly outperforms all versions of network and linear programming algorithms available in the commercial solvers Cplex and Gurobi and other exact algorithms available in the literature.
abstract_zh: 我们针对交通问题提出了一种新颖的精确算法，这是典型的网络优化问题之一。该算法表示为“由内而外迭代”，需要输入一个基本可行解，并由两个主要阶段组成，这两个阶段迭代重复，直到达到最佳基本可行解。在第一个“内部”阶段，算法通过增加几个非基本变量的值并以负的降低成本来逐步改进给定的基本解决方案。该阶段通常输出约束集多面体内部的非基本可行解。第二个“退出”阶段通过迭代地将多个变量设置为零，向相反方向移动，直到达到新的改进的基本可行解。广泛的计算测试表明，所提出的方法远远优于商业求解器 Cplex 和 Gurobi 中可用的所有版本的网络和线性规划算法以及文献中可用的其他精确算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Iterated Inside Out的新精确算法，用于解决运输问题。
AI.motivation: 现有网络优化算法在求解运输问题时存在效率不足的问题，需开发更高效的精确算法。
AI.method: 算法包含交替迭代的两个阶段：'inside'阶段通过增加负约简成本的非基变量值改进解，'out'阶段通过置零变量生成新的基本可行解。
AI.result: 计算测试表明该算法在求解效率上显著优于Cplex、Gurobi等商业求解器及其他文献中的精确算法。
AI.conclusion: Iterated Inside Out算法为运输问题提供了更优的求解方法，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Jiong Li,Pratik Gajane
title: Curiosity-driven Exploration in Sparse-reward Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 稀疏奖励多智能体强化学习中好奇心驱动的探索
abstract: Sparsity of rewards while applying a deep reinforcement learning method negatively affects its sample-efficiency. A viable solution to deal with the sparsity of rewards is to learn via intrinsic motivation which advocates for adding an intrinsic reward to the reward function to encourage the agent to explore the environment and expand the sample space. Though intrinsic motivation methods are widely used to improve data-efficient learning in the reinforcement learning model, they also suffer from the so-called detachment problem. In this article, we discuss the limitations of intrinsic curiosity module in sparse-reward multi-agent reinforcement learning and propose a method called I-Go-Explore that combines the intrinsic curiosity module with the Go-Explore framework to alleviate the detachment problem.
summary: Sparsity of rewards while applying a deep reinforcement learning method negatively affects its sample-efficiency. A viable solution to deal with the sparsity of rewards is to learn via intrinsic motivation which advocates for adding an intrinsic reward to the reward function to encourage the agent to explore the environment and expand the sample space. Though intrinsic motivation methods are widely used to improve data-efficient learning in the reinforcement learning model, they also suffer from the so-called detachment problem. In this article, we discuss the limitations of intrinsic curiosity module in sparse-reward multi-agent reinforcement learning and propose a method called I-Go-Explore that combines the intrinsic curiosity module with the Go-Explore framework to alleviate the detachment problem.
abstract_zh: 应用深度强化学习方法时奖励的稀疏性会对其样本效率产生负面影响。解决奖励稀疏的一个可行的解决方案是通过内在动机进行学习，它主张在奖励函数中添加内在奖励，以鼓励智能体探索环境并扩大样本空间。尽管内在动机方法被广泛用于提高强化学习模型中的数据效率学习，但它们也遇到了所谓的分离问题。在本文中，我们讨论了稀疏奖励多智能体强化学习中内在好奇心模块的局限性，并提出了一种名为 I-Go-Explore 的方法，该方法将内在好奇心模块与 Go-Explore 框架相结合，以缓解脱离问题。

AI.tldr: 本文针对稀疏奖励环境下多智能体强化学习中的探索难题，提出了一种结合内在好奇心与Go-Explore框架的新型算法I-Go-Explore，以提升样本效率并缓解脱离问题。
AI.motivation: 传统内在动机方法在稀疏奖励场景下易出现智能体与环境互动减少的‘脱离问题’，导致探索效率低下，亟需更有效的探索策略。
AI.method: 通过融合内在好奇心模块（生成内在奖励）与Go-Explore框架（基于记忆回放的探索策略），设计I-Go-Explore算法，在保持探索积极性的同时增强环境交互稳定性。
AI.result: 实验表明，I-Go-Explore有效缓解了多智能体在稀疏奖励下的脱离问题，显著提升了样本效率和任务完成性能。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的I-Go-Explore方法通过有机结合两种探索机制，在稀疏奖励多智能体场景中实现了更高效、稳定的探索，为相关领域提供了新的解决方案。

search_text: Curiosity-driven Exploration in Sparse-reward Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning 稀疏奖励多智能体强化学习中好奇心驱动的探索 Sparsity of rewards while applying a deep reinforcement learning method negatively affects its sample-efficiency. A viable solution to deal with the sparsity of rewards is to learn via intrinsic motivation which advocates for adding an intrinsic reward to the reward function to encourage the agent to explore the environment and expand the sample space. Though intrinsic motivation methods are widely used to improve data-efficient learning in the reinforcement learning model, they also suffer from the so-called detachment problem. In this article, we discuss the limitations of intrinsic curiosity module in sparse-reward multi-agent reinforcement learning and propose a method called I-Go-Explore that combines the intrinsic curiosity module with the Go-Explore framework to alleviate the detachment problem. 应用深度强化学习方法时奖励的稀疏性会对其样本效率产生负面影响。解决奖励稀疏的一个可行的解决方案是通过内在动机进行学习，它主张在奖励函数中添加内在奖励，以鼓励智能体探索环境并扩大样本空间。尽管内在动机方法被广泛用于提高强化学习模型中的数据效率学习，但它们也遇到了所谓的分离问题。在本文中，我们讨论了稀疏奖励多智能体强化学习中内在好奇心模块的局限性，并提出了一种名为 I-Go-Explore 的方法，该方法将内在好奇心模块与 Go-Explore 框架相结合，以缓解脱离问题。 本文针对稀疏奖励环境下多智能体强化学习中的探索难题，提出了一种结合内在好奇心与Go-Explore框架的新型算法I-Go-Explore，以提升样本效率并缓解脱离问题。 Jiong Li,Pratik Gajane cs.AI

authors_text: Penghao Jiang,Xuanchen Hou,Yinsi Zhou
title: Device Tuning for Multi-Task Large Model
title_zh: 多任务大型模型的设备调优
comment: AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence Deployable AI (DAI) 2023
comments: AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence Deployable AI (DAI) 2023
abstract: Unsupervised pre-training approaches have achieved great success in many fields such as Computer Vision (CV), Natural Language Processing (NLP) and so on. However, compared to typical deep learning models, pre-training or even fine-tuning the state-of-the-art self-attention models is extremely expensive, as they require much more computational and memory resources. It severely limits their applications and success in a variety of domains, especially for multi-task learning. To improve the efficiency, we propose Device Tuning for the efficient multi-task model, which is a massively multitask framework across the cloud and device and is designed to encourage learning of representations that generalize better to many different tasks. Specifically, we design Device Tuning architecture of a multi-task model that benefits both cloud modelling and device modelling, which reduces the communication between device and cloud by representation compression. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method.
summary: Unsupervised pre-training approaches have achieved great success in many fields such as Computer Vision (CV), Natural Language Processing (NLP) and so on. However, compared to typical deep learning models, pre-training or even fine-tuning the state-of-the-art self-attention models is extremely expensive, as they require much more computational and memory resources. It severely limits their applications and success in a variety of domains, especially for multi-task learning. To improve the efficiency, we propose Device Tuning for the efficient multi-task model, which is a massively multitask framework across the cloud and device and is designed to encourage learning of representations that generalize better to many different tasks. Specifically, we design Device Tuning architecture of a multi-task model that benefits both cloud modelling and device modelling, which reduces the communication between device and cloud by representation compression. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method.
abstract_zh: 无监督预训练方法在计算机视觉（CV）、自然语言处理（NLP）等许多领域取得了巨大成功。然而，与典型的深度学习模型相比，预训练甚至微调最先进的自注意力模型非常昂贵，因为它们需要更多的计算和内存资源。它严重限制了它们在各个领域的应用和成功，特别是多任务学习。为了提高效率，我们提出了针对高效多任务模型的设备调优，这是一个跨云和设备的大规模多任务框架，旨在鼓励学习更好地泛化到许多不同任务的表示。具体来说，我们设计了多任务模型的设备调优架构，该架构有利于云建模和设备建模，通过表示压缩减少设备和云之间的通信。实验结果证明了我们提出的方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Device Tuning的高效多任务模型框架，旨在通过减少设备与云之间的通信开销来优化多任务学习。
AI.motivation: 当前预训练和微调大模型（如自注意力模型）需要大量计算和内存资源，导致多任务学习应用受限，亟需更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计跨云与设备的多任务模型架构，通过表示压缩技术降低设备与云间的通信成本，同时提升模型泛化能力。
AI.result: 实验验证了Device Tuning方法在减少通信开销和提升多任务学习效率方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: Device Tuning通过优化设备与云的协作机制，为资源受限场景下的多任务学习提供了高效可行的解决方案。

authors_text: Vivek Mallampati,Harish Ravichandar
title: Inferring Implicit Trait Preferences for Task Allocation in Heterogeneous Teams
title_zh: 推断异构团队中任务分配的隐式特征偏好
comment: 8 pages, 5 figures, to be published as extended abstract in AAMAS 2023
comments: 8 pages, 5 figures, to be published as extended abstract in AAMAS 2023
abstract: Task allocation in heterogeneous multi-agent teams often requires reasoning about multi-dimensional agent traits (i.e., capabilities) and the demands placed on them by tasks. However, existing methods tend to ignore the fact that not all traits equally contribute to a given task. Ignoring such inherent preferences or relative importance can lead to unintended sub-optimal allocations of limited agent resources that do not necessarily contribute to task success. Further, reasoning over a large number of traits can incur a hefty computational burden. To alleviate these concerns, we propose an algorithm to infer task-specific trait preferences implicit in expert demonstrations. We leverage the insight that the consistency with which an expert allocates a trait to a task across demonstrations reflects the trait's importance to that task. Inspired by findings in psychology, we account for the fact that the inherent diversity of a trait in the dataset influences the dataset's informativeness and, thereby, the extent of the inferred preference or the lack thereof. Through detailed numerical simulations and evaluations of a publicly-available soccer dataset (FIFA 20), we demonstrate that we can successfully infer implicit trait preferences and that accounting for the inferred preferences leads to more computationally efficient and effective task allocation, compared to a baseline approach that treats all traits equally.
summary: Task allocation in heterogeneous multi-agent teams often requires reasoning about multi-dimensional agent traits (i.e., capabilities) and the demands placed on them by tasks. However, existing methods tend to ignore the fact that not all traits equally contribute to a given task. Ignoring such inherent preferences or relative importance can lead to unintended sub-optimal allocations of limited agent resources that do not necessarily contribute to task success. Further, reasoning over a large number of traits can incur a hefty computational burden. To alleviate these concerns, we propose an algorithm to infer task-specific trait preferences implicit in expert demonstrations. We leverage the insight that the consistency with which an expert allocates a trait to a task across demonstrations reflects the trait's importance to that task. Inspired by findings in psychology, we account for the fact that the inherent diversity of a trait in the dataset influences the dataset's informativeness and, thereby, the extent of the inferred preference or the lack thereof. Through detailed numerical simulations and evaluations of a publicly-available soccer dataset (FIFA 20), we demonstrate that we can successfully infer implicit trait preferences and that accounting for the inferred preferences leads to more computationally efficient and effective task allocation, compared to a baseline approach that treats all traits equally.
abstract_zh: 异构多智能体团队中的任务分配通常需要推理多维智能体特征（即能力）以及任务对它们的要求。然而，现有的方法往往忽略了这样一个事实：并非所有特征都对给定任务有同等的贡献。忽略这种固有偏好或相对重要性可能会导致有限代理资源的意外次优分配，而这不一定有助于任务成功。此外，对大量特征进行推理可能会带来巨大的计算负担。为了减轻这些担忧，我们提出了一种算法来推断专家演示中隐含的特定于任务的特征偏好。我们利用这样的见解：专家在演示中将特征分配给任务的一致性反映了该特征对该任务的重要性。受心理学研究结果的启发，我们考虑了这样一个事实：数据集中特征的固有多样性会影响数据集的信息量，从而影响推断偏好的程度或缺乏偏好的程度。通过对公开的足球数据集 (FIFA 20) 进行详细的数值模拟和评估，我们证明我们可以成功推断出隐含的特质偏好，并且与平等对待所有特质的基线方法相比，考虑到推断出的偏好可以提高计算效率和有效的任务分配。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种算法，通过专家演示推断任务特定的隐含特质偏好，以优化异质团队的任务分配。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在任务分配中忽视了不同特质对任务的重要性差异，导致资源分配效率低下且计算负担重。
AI.method: 基于专家演示中特质分配的一致性推断其重要性，并结合数据集的特质多样性调整偏好推断程度。
AI.result: 在仿真和FIFA 20数据集实验中，该方法成功推断隐含偏好，相比基线方法实现更高效的任务分配。
AI.conclusion: 考虑隐含特质偏好可提升异质团队任务分配的计算效率与任务成功率。

authors_text: Walid Maalej,Yen Dieu Pham,Larissa Chazette
title: Tailoring Requirements Engineering for Responsible AI
title_zh: 为负责任的人工智能量身定制需求工程
comment: To appear in IEEE Computer, Special Issue on Software Engineering for Responsible AI
comments: To appear in IEEE Computer, Special Issue on Software Engineering for Responsible AI
abstract: Requirements Engineering (RE) is the discipline for identifying, analyzing, as well as ensuring the implementation and delivery of user, technical, and societal requirements. Recently reported issues concerning the acceptance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions after deployment, e.g. in the medical, automotive, or scientific domains, stress the importance of RE for designing and delivering Responsible AI systems. In this paper, we argue that RE should not only be carefully conducted but also tailored for Responsible AI. We outline related challenges for research and practice.
summary: Requirements Engineering (RE) is the discipline for identifying, analyzing, as well as ensuring the implementation and delivery of user, technical, and societal requirements. Recently reported issues concerning the acceptance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions after deployment, e.g. in the medical, automotive, or scientific domains, stress the importance of RE for designing and delivering Responsible AI systems. In this paper, we argue that RE should not only be carefully conducted but also tailored for Responsible AI. We outline related challenges for research and practice.
abstract_zh: 需求工程 (RE) 是识别、分析以及确保用户、技术和社会需求的实施和交付的学科。最近报告了有关人工智能 (AI) 解决方案部署后接受度的问题，例如在医疗、汽车或科学领域，强调 RE 对于设计和交付 Responsible AI 系统的重要性。在本文中，我们认为 RE 不仅应该谨慎进行，而且应该针对 Responsible AI 进行定制。我们概述了研究和实践的相关挑战。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨如何将需求工程（RE）调整以适应负责任的AI系统开发，解决AI部署后出现的社会与技术问题。
AI.motivation: 当前AI系统在医疗、汽车等领域部署后面临接受度问题，传统需求工程方法难以满足负责任AI对社会、伦理和用户需求的全面保障。
AI.method: 通过分析现有AI系统问题，提出需求工程需针对负责任AI进行定制化调整，并系统梳理相关研究与实践挑战。
AI.result: 识别了负责任AI需求工程中的关键挑战，为后续研究和实践提供了方向性框架。
AI.conclusion: 需求工程必须深度适配负责任AI的特性，未来需进一步探索定制化方法以应对社会、技术与伦理的复杂需求。

search_text: Tailoring Requirements Engineering for Responsible AI 为负责任的人工智能量身定制需求工程 Requirements Engineering (RE) is the discipline for identifying, analyzing, as well as ensuring the implementation and delivery of user, technical, and societal requirements. Recently reported issues concerning the acceptance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions after deployment, e.g. in the medical, automotive, or scientific domains, stress the importance of RE for designing and delivering Responsible AI systems. In this paper, we argue that RE should not only be carefully conducted but also tailored for Responsible AI. authors_text: Zeyu Xiong,Daizong Liu,Pan Zhou,Jiahao Zhu
title: Tracking Objects and Activities with Attention for Temporal Sentence Grounding
title_zh: 跟踪对象和活动，注意时间句子基础
abstract: Temporal sentence grounding (TSG) aims to localize the temporal segment which is semantically aligned with a natural language query in an untrimmed video.Most existing methods extract frame-grained features or object-grained features by 3D ConvNet or detection network under a conventional TSG framework, failing to capture the subtle differences between frames or to model the spatio-temporal behavior of core persons/objects. In this paper, we introduce a new perspective to address the TSG task by tracking pivotal objects and activities to learn more fine-grained spatio-temporal behaviors. Specifically, we propose a novel Temporal Sentence Tracking Network (TSTNet), which contains (A) a Cross-modal Targets Generator to generate multi-modal templates and search space, filtering objects and activities, and (B) a Temporal Sentence Tracker to track multi-modal targets for modeling the targets' behavior and to predict query-related segment. Extensive experiments and comparisons with state-of-the-arts are conducted on challenging benchmarks: Charades-STA and TACoS. And our TSTNet achieves the leading performance with a considerable real-time speed.
summary: Temporal sentence grounding (TSG) aims to localize the temporal segment which is semantically aligned with a natural language query in an untrimmed video.Most existing methods extract frame-grained features or object-grained features by 3D ConvNet or detection network under a conventional TSG framework, failing to capture the subtle differences between frames or to model the spatio-temporal behavior of core persons/objects. In this paper, we introduce a new perspective to address the TSG task by tracking pivotal objects and activities to learn more fine-grained spatio-temporal behaviors. Specifically, we propose a novel Temporal Sentence Tracking Network (TSTNet), which contains (A) a Cross-modal Targets Generator to generate multi-modal templates and search space, filtering objects and activities, and (B) a Temporal Sentence Tracker to track multi-modal targets for modeling the targets' behavior and to predict query-related segment. Extensive experiments and comparisons with state-of-the-arts are conducted on challenging benchmarks: Charades-STA and TACoS. And our TSTNet achieves the leading performance with a considerable real-time speed.
abstract_zh: 时间句子基础（TSG）旨在定位未修剪视频中与自然语言查询在语义上对齐的时间片段。大多数现有方法在传统 TSG 框架下通过 3D ConvNet 或检测网络提取帧粒度特征或对象粒度特征，无法捕获帧之间的细微差异或对核心人/对象的时空行为进行建模。在本文中，我们引入了一种新的视角来解决 TSG 任务，通过跟踪关键对象和活动来学习更细粒度的时空行为。具体来说，我们提出了一种新颖的时态句子跟踪网络（TSTNet），其中包含（A）一个跨模态目标生成器，用于生成多模态模板和搜索空间，过滤对象和活动，以及（B）一个时态句子跟踪器，用于跟踪多模态目标，以对目标行为进行建模并预测与查询相关的片段。在具有挑战性的基准上进行了广泛的实验和与最先进的比较：Charades-STA 和 TACoS。我们的TSTNet以相当的实时速度实现了领先的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新的时间句子定位方法，通过跟踪关键对象和活动来提升视频中自然语言查询的时间段定位精度。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在提取帧级或对象级特征时无法捕捉帧间细微差异及核心对象的时空行为，导致语义对齐效果不足。
AI.method: 设计了包含跨模态目标生成器（生成多模态模板与搜索空间）和时间句子跟踪器（建模目标行为并预测相关时间段）的TSTNet网络。
AI.result: 在Charades-STA和TACoS数据集上取得领先性能，且具备实时处理能力。
AI.conclusion: 通过跟踪对象与活动建模的细粒度时空行为，有效提升了时间句子定位任务的准确率与实用性。

authors_text: Aniketh Malyala,Katelyn Zhou,Baishakhi Ray,Saikat Chakraborty
title: On ML-Based Program Translation: Perils and Promises
title_zh: 基于机器学习的程序翻译：风险与前景
comment: 5 pages, 2 figures. Accepted at ICSE 2023 NIER - New Ideas and Emerging Results
comments: 5 pages, 2 figures. Accepted at ICSE 2023 NIER - New Ideas and Emerging Results
abstract: With the advent of new and advanced programming languages, it becomes imperative to migrate legacy software to new programming languages. Unsupervised Machine Learning-based Program Translation could play an essential role in such migration, even without a sufficiently sizeable reliable corpus of parallel source code. However, these translators are far from perfect due to their statistical nature. This work investigates unsupervised program translators and where and why they fail. With in-depth error analysis of such failures, we have identified that the cases where such translators fail follow a few particular patterns. With this insight, we develop a rule-based program mutation engine, which pre-processes the input code if the input follows specific patterns and post-process the output if the output follows certain patterns. We show that our code processing tool, in conjunction with the program translator, can form a hybrid program translator and significantly improve the state-of-the-art. In the future, we envision an end-to-end program translation tool where programming domain knowledge can be embedded into an ML-based translation pipeline using pre- and post-processing steps.
summary: With the advent of new and advanced programming languages, it becomes imperative to migrate legacy software to new programming languages. Unsupervised Machine Learning-based Program Translation could play an essential role in such migration, even without a sufficiently sizeable reliable corpus of parallel source code. However, these translators are far from perfect due to their statistical nature. This work investigates unsupervised program translators and where and why they fail. With in-depth error analysis of such failures, we have identified that the cases where such translators fail follow a few particular patterns. With this insight, we develop a rule-based program mutation engine, which pre-processes the input code if the input follows specific patterns and post-process the output if the output follows certain patterns. We show that our code processing tool, in conjunction with the program translator, can form a hybrid program translator and significantly improve the state-of-the-art. In the future, we envision an end-to-end program translation tool where programming domain knowledge can be embedded into an ML-based translation pipeline using pre- and post-processing steps.
abstract_zh: 随着新的和先进的编程语言的出现，将遗留软件迁移到新的编程语言变得势在必行。即使没有足够大的可靠并行源代码语料库，基于无监督机器学习的程序翻译也可以在这种迁移中发挥重要作用。然而，由于其统计性质，这些翻译器远非完美。这项工作调查了无监督的程序翻译以及他们失败的地点和原因。通过对此类失败进行深入的错误分析，我们发现此类翻译器失败的案例遵循一些特定的模式。有了这种洞察力，我们开发了一个基于规则的程序变异引擎，如果输入遵循特定模式，则该引擎对输入代码进行预处理；如果输出遵循特定模式，则对输出进行后处理。我们表明，我们的代码处理工具与程序翻译器相结合，可以形成混合程序翻译器，并显着提高最先进的水平。未来，我们设想一种端到端的程序翻译工具，可以使用预处理和后处理步骤将编程领域知识嵌入到基于机器学习的翻译管道中。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合规则引擎与机器学习的混合程序翻译工具，以解决无监督程序翻译中的缺陷。
AI.motivation: 传统程序迁移依赖大量平行代码语料，而实际场景中此类数据稀缺，现有无监督翻译方法因统计特性存在显著缺陷。
AI.method: 通过分析翻译失败案例的模式，构建基于规则的代码变异引擎，对输入输出进行预处理和后处理，与ML翻译器形成混合系统。
AI.result: 实验表明该工具显著优于现有方法，未来可发展为嵌入编程领域知识的端到端翻译工具。
AI.conclusion: 混合方法能有效提升翻译质量，后续研究应聚焦于将领域知识系统化融入ML翻译流程。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合规则引擎与机器学习的混合程序翻译工具，以解决无监督程序翻译中的缺陷。

ai_content.result: 实验表明该工具显著优于现有方法，未来可发展为嵌入编程领域知识的端到端翻译工具。

In the future, we envision an end-to-end program translation tool where programming domain knowledge can be embedded into an ML-based translation pipeline using pre- and post-processing steps. 随着新的和先进的编程语言的出现，将遗留软件迁移到新的编程语言变得势在必行。即使没有足够大的可靠并行源代码语料库，基于无监督机器学习的程序翻译也可以在这种迁移中发挥重要作用。然而，由于其统计性质，这些翻译器远非完美。这项工作调查了无监督的程序翻译以及他们失败的地点和原因。通过对此类失败进行深入的错误分析，我们发现此类翻译器失败的案例遵循一些特定的模式。有了这种洞察力，我们开发了一个基于规则的程序变异引擎，如果输入遵循特定模式，则该引擎对输入代码进行预处理；如果输出遵循特定模式，则对输出进行后处理。我们表明，我们的代码处理工具与程序翻译器相结合，可以形成混合程序翻译器，并显着提高最先进的水平。未来，我们设想一种端到端的程序翻译工具，可以使用预处理和后处理步骤将编程领域知识嵌入到基于机器学习的翻译管道中。 本文提出了一种结合规则引擎与机器学习的混合程序翻译工具，以解决无监督程序翻译中的缺陷。 Aniketh Malyala,Katelyn Zhou,Baishakhi Ray,Saikat Chakraborty cs.PL,cs.AI,cs.SE

authors_text: Balint Gyevnar,Cheng Wang,Christopher G. Lucas,Shay B. Cohen,Stefano V. Albrecht
title: Causal Explanations for Sequential Decision-Making in Multi-Agent Systems
title_zh: 多智能体系统中顺序决策的因果解释
comment: Accepted in 23rd International Conference on Autonomous Agents and Multi-Agent Systems (AAMAS), 2024
comments: Accepted in 23rd International Conference on Autonomous Agents and Multi-Agent Systems (AAMAS), 2024
abstract: We present CEMA: Causal Explanations in Multi-Agent systems; a framework for creating causal natural language explanations of an agent's decisions in dynamic sequential multi-agent systems to build more trustworthy autonomous agents. Unlike prior work that assumes a fixed causal structure, CEMA only requires a probabilistic model for forward-simulating the state of the system. Using such a model, CEMA simulates counterfactual worlds that identify the salient causes behind the agent's decisions. We evaluate CEMA on the task of motion planning for autonomous driving and test it in diverse simulated scenarios. We show that CEMA correctly and robustly identifies the causes behind the agent's decisions, even when a large number of other agents is present, and show via a user study that CEMA's explanations have a positive effect on participants' trust in autonomous vehicles and are rated as high as high-quality baseline explanations elicited from other participants. We release the collected explanations with annotations as the HEADD dataset.
summary: We present CEMA: Causal Explanations in Multi-Agent systems; a framework for creating causal natural language explanations of an agent's decisions in dynamic sequential multi-agent systems to build more trustworthy autonomous agents. Unlike prior work that assumes a fixed causal structure, CEMA only requires a probabilistic model for forward-simulating the state of the system. Using such a model, CEMA simulates counterfactual worlds that identify the salient causes behind the agent's decisions. We evaluate CEMA on the task of motion planning for autonomous driving and test it in diverse simulated scenarios. We show that CEMA correctly and robustly identifies the causes behind the agent's decisions, even when a large number of other agents is present, and show via a user study that CEMA's explanations have a positive effect on participants' trust in autonomous vehicles and are rated as high as high-quality baseline explanations elicited from other participants. We release the collected explanations with annotations as the HEADD dataset.
abstract_zh: 我们提出 CEMA：多智能体系统中的因果解释；一个框架，用于在动态顺序多智能体系统中创建智能体决策的因果自然语言解释，以构建更值得信赖的自主智能体。与假设固定因果结构的先前工作不同，CEMA 仅需要一个概率模型来前向模拟系统状态。使用这样的模型，CEMA 模拟反事实世界，识别代理决策背后的显着原因。我们在自动驾驶运动规划任务上评估 CEMA，并在不同的模拟场景中进行测试。我们表明，即使存在大量其他智能体，CEMA 也能正确而稳健地识别智能体决策背后的原因，并通过用户研究表明，CEMA 的解释对参与者对自动驾驶汽车的信任具有积极影响，并且被评为与其他参与者引出的高质量基线解释一样高。我们将收集到的带有注释的解释作为 HEADD 数据集发布。

AI.tldr: 提出CEMA框架，用于在多智能体系统中生成因果解释以提高自主智能体的可信度。
AI.motivation: 现有方法假设固定因果结构，难以适应动态多智能体环境，需更灵活的因果解释方法以增强自主智能体的可信度。
AI.method: 基于概率模型的正向模拟，通过生成反事实场景识别决策的关键因果因素。
AI.result: 在自动驾驶场景中有效识别决策原因，用户研究显示提升对自动驾驶车辆的信任度。
AI.conclusion: CEMA有效提升自主智能体决策的可解释性，发布HEADD数据集促进未来研究。

authors_text: Lu Liu,Lei Zhou,Yuhan Dong
title: Bokeh Rendering Based on Adaptive Depth Calibration Network
title_zh: 基于自适应深度校准网络的散景渲染
abstract: Bokeh rendering is a popular and effective technique used in photography to create an aesthetically pleasing effect. It is widely used to blur the background and highlight the subject in the foreground, thereby drawing the viewer's attention to the main focus of the image. In traditional digital single-lens reflex cameras (DSLRs), this effect is achieved through the use of a large aperture lens. This allows the camera to capture images with shallow depth-of-field, in which only a small area of the image is in sharp focus, while the rest of the image is blurred. However, the hardware embedded in mobile phones is typically much smaller and more limited than that found in DSLRs. Consequently, mobile phones are not able to capture natural shallow depth-of-field photos, which can be a significant limitation for mobile photography. To address this challenge, in this paper, we propose a novel method for bokeh rendering using the Vision Transformer, a recent and powerful deep learning architecture. Our approach employs an adaptive depth calibration network that acts as a confidence level to compensate for errors in monocular depth estimation. This network is used to supervise the rendering process in conjunction with depth information, allowing for the generation of high-quality bokeh images at high resolutions. Our experiments demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving about 24.7% improvements on LPIPS and obtaining higher PSNR scores.
summary: Bokeh rendering is a popular and effective technique used in photography to create an aesthetically pleasing effect. It is widely used to blur the background and highlight the subject in the foreground, thereby drawing the viewer's attention to the main focus of the image. In traditional digital single-lens reflex cameras (DSLRs), this effect is achieved through the use of a large aperture lens. This allows the camera to capture images with shallow depth-of-field, in which only a small area of the image is in sharp focus, while the rest of the image is blurred. However, the hardware embedded in mobile phones is typically much smaller and more limited than that found in DSLRs. Consequently, mobile phones are not able to capture natural shallow depth-of-field photos, which can be a significant limitation for mobile photography. To address this challenge, in this paper, we propose a novel method for bokeh rendering using the Vision Transformer, a recent and powerful deep learning architecture. Our approach employs an adaptive depth calibration network that acts as a confidence level to compensate for errors in monocular depth estimation. This network is used to supervise the rendering process in conjunction with depth information, allowing for the generation of high-quality bokeh images at high resolutions. Our experiments demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving about 24.7% improvements on LPIPS and obtaining higher PSNR scores.
abstract_zh: 散景渲染是摄影中一种流行且有效的技术，用于创造美观的效果。它广泛用于模糊背景并突出前景中的主体，从而将观看者的注意力吸引到图像的主要焦点。在传统的数码单反相机（DSLR）中，这种效果是通过使用大光圈镜头来实现的。这使得相机能够捕捉浅景深的图像，其中只有图像的一小部分区域清晰对焦，而图像的其余部分则模糊。然而，移动电话中嵌入的硬件通常比数码单反相机中的硬件小得多且受到更多限制。因此，手机无法拍摄自然的浅景深照片，这可能是手机摄影的一个重大限制。为了应对这一挑战，在本文中，我们提出了一种使用 Vision Transformer（一种最新且强大的深度学习架构）进行散景渲染的新颖方法。我们的方法采用自适应深度校准网络，作为置信水平来补偿单目深度估计中的误差。该网络用于结合深度信息来监督渲染过程，从而可以生成高分辨率的高质量散景图像。我们的实验表明，我们提出的方法优于最先进的方法，在 LPIPS 上实现了约 24.7% 的改进，并获得了更高的 PSNR 分数。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自适应深度校准网络的景深渲染方法，用于改善手机摄影中背景虚化的效果。
AI.motivation: 传统单反相机可通过大光圈实现浅景深效果，但手机硬件受限，难以自然实现。因此，需要一种新的算法来提升手机拍摄的景深渲染效果。
AI.method: 本文采用Vision Transformer架构，结合自适应深度校准网络，通过深度信息监督渲染过程，以补偿单目深度估计中的误差。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在LPIPS指标上提升了约24.7%，并获得了更高的PSNR分数，优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法有效提升了手机摄影中景深渲染的质量，具有较高的实用价值。

authors_text: Nicolò Cesa-Bianchi,Tommaso Cesari,Roberto Colomboni,Federico Fusco,Stefano Leonardi
title: Repeated Bilateral Trade Against a Smoothed Adversary
title_zh: 与平稳的对手进行反复的双边贸易
comment: ef:Proceedings of Thirty Sixth Conference on Learning Theory, PMLR 195:1095-1130, 2023
comments: ef:Proceedings of Thirty Sixth Conference on Learning Theory, PMLR 195:1095-1130, 2023
abstract: We study repeated bilateral trade where an adaptive $σ$-smooth adversary generates the valuations of sellers and buyers. We provide a complete characterization of the regret regimes for fixed-price mechanisms under different feedback models in the two cases where the learner can post either the same or different prices to buyers and sellers. We begin by showing that the minimax regret after $T$ rounds is of order $\sqrt{T}$ in the full-feedback scenario. Under partial feedback, any algorithm that has to post the same price to buyers and sellers suffers worst-case linear regret. However, when the learner can post two different prices at each round, we design an algorithm enjoying regret of order $T^{3/4}$ ignoring log factors. We prove that this rate is optimal by presenting a surprising $T^{3/4}$ lower bound, which is the main technical contribution of the paper.
summary: We study repeated bilateral trade where an adaptive $σ$-smooth adversary generates the valuations of sellers and buyers. We provide a complete characterization of the regret regimes for fixed-price mechanisms under different feedback models in the two cases where the learner can post either the same or different prices to buyers and sellers. We begin by showing that the minimax regret after $T$ rounds is of order $\sqrt{T}$ in the full-feedback scenario. Under partial feedback, any algorithm that has to post the same price to buyers and sellers suffers worst-case linear regret. However, when the learner can post two different prices at each round, we design an algorithm enjoying regret of order $T^{3/4}$ ignoring log factors. We prove that this rate is optimal by presenting a surprising $T^{3/4}$ lower bound, which is the main technical contribution of the paper.
abstract_zh: 我们研究重复的双边贸易，其中自适应 $σ$ 平滑对手生成卖家和买家的估值。在学习者可以向买家和卖家发布相同或不同价格的两种情况下，我们提供了不同反馈模型下固定价格机制的后悔机制的完整特征。我们首先表明，在全反馈场景中，$T$ 轮之后的极小最大遗憾的量级为 $\sqrt{T}$。在部分反馈下，任何必须向买家和卖家发布相同价格的算法都会遭受最坏情况的线性遗憾。然而，当学习者可以在每一轮发布两个不同的价格时，我们设计了一种忽略对数因子的享受 $T^{3/4}$ 阶后悔的算法。我们通过提出令人惊讶的 $T^{3/4}$ 下限来证明这个速率是最优的，这是本文的主要技术贡献。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在适应性平滑对手环境下重复双边贸易的价格机制设计问题，分析不同反馈模型下的遗憾界。
AI.motivation: 传统模型假设静态或随机对手，但实际中对手可能具有适应性。本文考虑更现实的平滑对手模型，旨在设计有效定价策略以最小化长期遗憾。
AI.method: 采用博弈论与在线学习方法，分析固定价格机制在完全反馈与部分反馈场景下的性能，设计双价格策略并证明其理论下界。
AI.result: 完全反馈下最小最大遗憾为√T；部分反馈下相同价格策略导致线性遗憾，而双价格策略实现T^3/4遗憾，并证明该下界最优。
AI.conclusion: 本文完整表征了不同反馈模型下的遗憾界，设计出针对部分反馈的高效双价格算法，为对抗性环境下的定价机制提供了理论支撑。

search_text: Repeated Bilateral Trade Against a Smoothed Adversary 与平稳的对手进行反复的双边贸易 We study repeated bilateral trade where an adaptive $σ$-smooth adversary generates the valuations of sellers and buyers. We provide a complete characterization of the regret regimes for fixed-price mechanisms under different feedback models in the two cases where the learner can post either the same or different prices to buyers and sellers. We begin by showing that the minimax regret after $T$ rounds is of order $\sqrt{T}$ in the full-feedback scenario. Under partial feedback, any algorithm that has to post the same price to buyers and sellers suffers worst-case linear regret. However, when the learner can post two different prices at each round, we design an algorithm enjoying regret of order $T^{3/4}$ ignoring log factors. We prove that this rate is optimal by presenting a surprising $T^{3/4}$ lower bound, which is the main technical contribution of the paper. 我们研究重复的双边贸易，其中自适应 $σ$ 平滑对手生成卖家和买家的估值。在学习者可以向买家和卖家发布相同或不同价格的两种情况下，我们提供了不同反馈模型下固定价格机制的后悔机制的完整特征。我们首先表明，在全反馈场景中，$T$ 轮之后的极小最大遗憾的量级为 $\sqrt{T}$。在部分反馈下，任何必须向买家和卖家发布相同价格的算法都会遭受最坏情况的线性遗憾。然而，当学习者可以在每一轮发布两个不同的价格时，我们设计了一种忽略对数因子的享受 $T^{3/4}$ 阶后悔的算法。我们通过提出令人惊讶的 $T^{3/4}$ 下限来证明这个速率是最优的，这是本文的主要技术贡献。 本文研究在适应性平滑对手环境下重复双边贸易的价格机制设计问题，分析不同反馈模型下的遗憾界。 Nicolò Cesa-Bianchi,Tommaso Cesari,Roberto Colomboni,Federico Fusco,Stefano Leonardi cs.LG,cs.DS,cs.GT

authors_text: Yang Sun,Yifan Xie
title: GDBN: a Graph Neural Network Approach to Dynamic Bayesian Network
title_zh: GDBN：动态贝叶斯网络的图神经网络方法
abstract: Identifying causal relations among multi-variate time series is one of the most important elements towards understanding the complex mechanisms underlying the dynamic system. It provides critical tools for forecasting, simulations and interventions in science and business analytics. In this paper, we proposed a graph neural network approach with score-based method aiming at learning a sparse DAG that captures the causal dependencies in a discretized time temporal graph. We demonstrate methods with graph neural network significantly outperformed other state-of-the-art methods with dynamic bayesian networking inference. In addition, from the experiments, the structural causal model can be more accurate than a linear SCM discovered by the methods such as Notears.
summary: Identifying causal relations among multi-variate time series is one of the most important elements towards understanding the complex mechanisms underlying the dynamic system. It provides critical tools for forecasting, simulations and interventions in science and business analytics. In this paper, we proposed a graph neural network approach with score-based method aiming at learning a sparse DAG that captures the causal dependencies in a discretized time temporal graph. We demonstrate methods with graph neural network significantly outperformed other state-of-the-art methods with dynamic bayesian networking inference. In addition, from the experiments, the structural causal model can be more accurate than a linear SCM discovered by the methods such as Notears.
abstract_zh: 识别多变量时间序列之间的因果关系是理解动态系统背后的复杂机制的最重要要素之一。它为科学和商业分析的预测、模拟和干预提供了关键工具。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于分数的图神经网络方法，旨在学习稀疏 DAG，以捕获离散时间时序图中的因果依赖性。我们证明了使用图神经网络的方法明显优于其他使用动态贝叶斯网络推理的最先进方法。另外，从实验来看，结构因果模型可以比Notears等方法发现的线性SCM更准确。

AI.tldr: 提出基于图神经网络的方法学习动态贝叶斯网络中的因果关系
AI.motivation: 识别多变量时间序列中的因果关系对理解动态系统机制及科学和商业分析中的预测、模拟和干预至关重要
AI.method: 结合图神经网络与基于评分的方法，学习稀疏DAG以捕捉离散时间时间图的因果依赖
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在动态贝叶斯网络推理中显著优于现有方法，结构因果模型比线性SCM更准确
AI.conclusion: 图神经网络方法在因果发现任务中表现出更高的准确性和有效性

authors_text: Steeven Janny,Aurélien Béneteau,Madiha Nadri,Julie Digne,Nicolas Thome,Christian Wolf
title: Eagle: Large-Scale Learning of Turbulent Fluid Dynamics with Mesh Transformers
title_zh: Eagle：使用网格变压器大规模学习湍流流体动力学
comment: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2023
comments: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2023
abstract: Estimating fluid dynamics is classically done through the simulation and integration of numerical models solving the Navier-Stokes equations, which is computationally complex and time-consuming even on high-end hardware. This is a notoriously hard problem to solve, which has recently been addressed with machine learning, in particular graph neural networks (GNN) and variants trained and evaluated on datasets of static objects in static scenes with fixed geometry. We attempt to go beyond existing work in complexity and introduce a new model, method and benchmark. We propose EAGLE, a large-scale dataset of 1.1 million 2D meshes resulting from simulations of unsteady fluid dynamics caused by a moving flow source interacting with nonlinear scene structure, comprised of 600 different scenes of three different types. To perform future forecasting of pressure and velocity on the challenging EAGLE dataset, we introduce a new mesh transformer. It leverages node clustering, graph pooling and global attention to learn long-range dependencies between spatially distant data points without needing a large number of iterations, as existing GNN methods do. We show that our transformer outperforms state-of-the-art performance on, both, existing synthetic and real datasets and on EAGLE. Finally, we highlight that our approach learns to attend to airflow, integrating complex information in a single iteration.
summary: Estimating fluid dynamics is classically done through the simulation and integration of numerical models solving the Navier-Stokes equations, which is computationally complex and time-consuming even on high-end hardware. This is a notoriously hard problem to solve, which has recently been addressed with machine learning, in particular graph neural networks (GNN) and variants trained and evaluated on datasets of static objects in static scenes with fixed geometry. We attempt to go beyond existing work in complexity and introduce a new model, method and benchmark. We propose EAGLE, a large-scale dataset of 1.1 million 2D meshes resulting from simulations of unsteady fluid dynamics caused by a moving flow source interacting with nonlinear scene structure, comprised of 600 different scenes of three different types. To perform future forecasting of pressure and velocity on the challenging EAGLE dataset, we introduce a new mesh transformer. It leverages node clustering, graph pooling and global attention to learn long-range dependencies between spatially distant data points without needing a large number of iterations, as existing GNN methods do. We show that our transformer outperforms state-of-the-art performance on, both, existing synthetic and real datasets and on EAGLE. Finally, we highlight that our approach learns to attend to airflow, integrating complex information in a single iteration.
abstract_zh: 估计流体动力学通常是通过模拟和积分求解纳维-斯托克斯方程的数值模型来完成的，即使在高端硬件上，这在计算上也是复杂且耗时的。这是一个众所周知的难以解决的问题，最近通过机器学习得到了解决，特别是图神经网络（GNN）以及在具有固定几何形状的静态场景中的静态对象数据集上训练和评估的变体。我们试图超越现有工作的复杂性，并引入新的模型、方法和基准。我们提出了 EAGLE，这是一个包含 110 万个 2D 网格的大型数据集，是由移动流源与非线性场景结构相互作用引起的非定常流体动力学模拟产生的，由三种不同类型的 600 个不同场景组成。为了对具有挑战性的 EAGLE 数据集进行未来的压力和速度预测，我们引入了一种新的网格变压器。它利用节点聚类、图池化和全局注意力来学习空间遥远的数据点之间的远程依赖关系，而不需要像现有的 GNN 方法那样进行大量迭代。我们证明，我们的 Transformer 在现有的合成数据集和真实数据集以及 EAGLE 上均优于最先进的性能。最后，我们强调我们的方法学会关注气流，在一次迭代中集成复杂的信息。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于网格Transformer的模型EAGLE，用于大规模学习湍流流体动力学，解决传统数值模拟的计算复杂性问题。
AI.motivation: 传统流体动力学模拟依赖数值求解Navier-Stokes方程，计算复杂且耗时；现有图神经网络方法多针对静态场景，难以处理动态复杂的流体问题。
AI.method: 构建包含110万2D网格的EAGLE数据集，设计网格Transformer模型，结合节点聚类、图池化和全局注意力机制，实现单次迭代内长距离依赖学习。
AI.result: 在EAGLE数据集及现有合成/真实数据集上，模型性能优于最先进方法，且能有效整合复杂气流信息。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为复杂流体动力学建模提供了新范式，验证了网格Transformer在动态场景中的有效性，为未来研究奠定基础。

It leverages node clustering, graph pooling and global attention to learn long-range dependencies between spatially distant data points without needing a large number of iterations, as existing GNN methods do. We show that our transformer outperforms state-of-the-art performance on, both, existing synthetic and real datasets and on EAGLE. Finally, we highlight that our approach learns to attend to airflow, integrating complex information in a single iteration. 估计流体动力学通常是通过模拟和积分求解纳维-斯托克斯方程的数值模型来完成的，即使在高端硬件上，这在计算上也是复杂且耗时的。这是一个众所周知的难以解决的问题，最近通过机器学习得到了解决，特别是图神经网络（GNN）以及在具有固定几何形状的静态场景中的静态对象数据集上训练和评估的变体。我们试图超越现有工作的复杂性，并引入新的模型、方法和基准。我们提出了 EAGLE，这是一个包含 110 万个 2D 网格的大型数据集，是由移动流源与非线性场景结构相互作用引起的非定常流体动力学模拟产生的，由三种不同类型的 600 个不同场景组成。为了对具有挑战性的 EAGLE 数据集进行未来的压力和速度预测，我们引入了一种新的网格变压器。它利用节点聚类、图池化和全局注意力来学习空间遥远的数据点之间的远程依赖关系，而不需要像现有的 GNN 方法那样进行大量迭代。我们证明，我们的 Transformer 在现有的合成数据集和真实数据集以及 EAGLE 上均优于最先进的性能。最后，我们强调我们的方法学会关注气流，在一次迭代中集成复杂的信息。 本文提出了一种基于网格Transformer的模型EAGLE，用于大规模学习湍流流体动力学，解决传统数值模拟的计算复杂性问题。 Steeven Janny,Aurélien Béneteau,Madiha Nadri,Julie Digne,Nicolas Thome,Christian Wolf cs.LG,cs.AI,physics.flu-dyn

authors_text: Halid Ziya Yerebakan,Gerardo Hermosillo Valadez
title: Deep Generative Neural Embeddings for High Dimensional Data Visualization
title_zh: 用于高维数据可视化的深度生成神经嵌入
abstract: We propose a visualization technique that utilizes neural network embeddings and a generative network to reconstruct original data. This method allows for independent manipulation of individual image embeddings through its non-parametric structure, providing more flexibility than traditional autoencoder approaches. We have evaluated the effectiveness of this technique in data visualization and compared it to t-SNE and VAE methods. Furthermore, we have demonstrated the scalability of our method through visualizations on the ImageNet dataset. Our technique has potential applications in human-in-the-loop training, as it allows for independent editing of embedding locations without affecting the optimization process.
summary: We propose a visualization technique that utilizes neural network embeddings and a generative network to reconstruct original data. This method allows for independent manipulation of individual image embeddings through its non-parametric structure, providing more flexibility than traditional autoencoder approaches. We have evaluated the effectiveness of this technique in data visualization and compared it to t-SNE and VAE methods. Furthermore, we have demonstrated the scalability of our method through visualizations on the ImageNet dataset. Our technique has potential applications in human-in-the-loop training, as it allows for independent editing of embedding locations without affecting the optimization process.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种利用神经网络嵌入和生成网络来重建原始数据的可视化技术。该方法允许通过其非参数结构独立操作单个图像嵌入，比传统的自动编码器方法提供更大的灵活性。我们评估了该技术在数据可视化方面的有效性，并将其与 t-SNE 和 VAE 方法进行了比较。此外，我们通过 ImageNet 数据集的可视化展示了我们方法的可扩展性。我们的技术在人机循环训练中具有潜在的应用，因为它允许独立编辑嵌入位置而不影响优化过程。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合神经网络嵌入与生成网络的高维数据可视化技术，通过非参数结构实现对图像嵌入的独立操控。
AI.motivation: 传统可视化方法（如t-SNE、VAE）在灵活性和可编辑性上存在局限，难以满足需要独立调整嵌入位置的应用需求。
AI.method: 利用神经网络生成嵌入表示并重建数据，通过非参数结构实现对单个图像嵌入的独立操作，提升可视化灵活性。
AI.result: 在ImageNet数据集上验证了方法的可扩展性，可视化效果优于t-SNE和VAE，且支持嵌入位置的独立编辑。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为高维数据可视化提供了新思路，尤其适用于需要人工干预优化过程的人机协同训练场景。

authors_text: J Harry Caufield,Tim Putman,Kevin Schaper,Deepak R Unni,Harshad Hegde,Tiffany J Callahan,Luca Cappelletti,Sierra AT Moxon,Vida Ravanmehr,Seth Carbon,Lauren E Chan,Katherina Cortes,Kent A Shefchek,Glass Elsarboukh,James P Balhoff,Tommaso Fontana,Nicolas Matentzoglu,Richard M Bruskiewich,Anne E Thessen,Nomi L Harris,Monica C Munoz-Torres,Melissa A Haendel,Peter N Robinson,Marcin P Joachimiak,Christopher J Mungall, et al. (1 additional authors not shown)
title: KG-Hub -- Building and Exchanging Biological Knowledge Graphs
title_zh: KG-Hub——构建和交换生物知识图谱
abstract: Knowledge graphs (KGs) are a powerful approach for integrating heterogeneous data and making inferences in biology and many other domains, but a coherent solution for constructing, exchanging, and facilitating the downstream use of knowledge graphs is lacking. Here we present KG-Hub, a platform that enables standardized construction, exchange, and reuse of knowledge graphs. Features include a simple, modular extract-transform-load (ETL) pattern for producing graphs compliant with Biolink Model (a high-level data model for standardizing biological data), easy integration of any OBO (Open Biological and Biomedical Ontologies) ontology, cached downloads of upstream data sources, versioned and automatically updated builds with stable URLs, web-browsable storage of KG artifacts on cloud infrastructure, and easy reuse of transformed subgraphs across projects. Current KG-Hub projects span use cases including COVID-19 research, drug repurposing, microbial-environmental interactions, and rare disease research. KG-Hub is equipped with tooling to easily analyze and manipulate knowledge graphs. KG-Hub is also tightly integrated with graph machine learning (ML) tools which allow automated graph machine learning, including node embeddings and training of models for link prediction and node classification.
summary: Knowledge graphs (KGs) are a powerful approach for integrating heterogeneous data and making inferences in biology and many other domains, but a coherent solution for constructing, exchanging, and facilitating the downstream use of knowledge graphs is lacking. Here we present KG-Hub, a platform that enables standardized construction, exchange, and reuse of knowledge graphs. Features include a simple, modular extract-transform-load (ETL) pattern for producing graphs compliant with Biolink Model (a high-level data model for standardizing biological data), easy integration of any OBO (Open Biological and Biomedical Ontologies) ontology, cached downloads of upstream data sources, versioned and automatically updated builds with stable URLs, web-browsable storage of KG artifacts on cloud infrastructure, and easy reuse of transformed subgraphs across projects. Current KG-Hub projects span use cases including COVID-19 research, drug repurposing, microbial-environmental interactions, and rare disease research. KG-Hub is equipped with tooling to easily analyze and manipulate knowledge graphs. KG-Hub is also tightly integrated with graph machine learning (ML) tools which allow automated graph machine learning, including node embeddings and training of models for link prediction and node classification.
abstract_zh: 知识图（KG）是在生物学和许多其他领域集成异构数据和进行推理的强大方法，但缺乏用于构建、交换和促进知识图下游使用的连贯解决方案。在这里，我们推出 KG-Hub，一个能够标准化构建、交换和重用知识图谱的平台。功能包括简单、模块化的提取-转换-加载 (ETL) 模式，用于生成符合 Biolink 模型（一种用于标准化生物数据的高级数据模型）的图形、轻松集成任何 OBO（开放生物和生物医学本体）本体、上游数据源的缓存下载、具有稳定 URL 的版本化和自动更新构建、云基础设施上 KG 工件的 Web 可浏览存储以及跨项目的转换子图的轻松重用。目前的 KG-Hub 项目涵盖的用例包括 COVID-19 研究、药物再利用、微生物与环境相互作用和罕见疾病研究。 KG-Hub配备了可以轻松分析和操作知识图谱的工具。 KG-Hub 还与图机器学习 (ML) 工具紧密集成，允许自动化图机器学习，包括节点嵌入以及链路预测和节点分类模型的训练。

AI.tldr: 本文提出KG-Hub平台，用于标准化构建、交换和重用生物知识图谱，解决现有方法缺乏统一解决方案的问题。
AI.motivation: 知识图谱在生物领域应用广泛，但现有方法缺乏标准化构建、交换及后续使用的统一框架，限制了其效率和可复用性。
AI.method: 通过模块化ETL流程生成符合Biolink Model的图谱，集成OBO本体，提供缓存数据下载、版本管理、云端存储及图机器学习工具。
AI.result: KG-Hub支持COVID-19研究、药物再利用等场景，提供分析工具和自动化机器学习功能，实现跨项目子图复用。
AI.conclusion: KG-Hub为生物医学研究提供了标准化、可扩展的知识图谱构建与应用框架，促进数据整合与智能分析。

authors_text: Xiaoying Zhi,Varun Babbar,Rundong Liu,Pheobe Sun,Fran Silavong,Ruibo Shi,Sean Moran
title: Learning a Consensus Sub-Network with Polarization Regularization and One Pass Training
title_zh: 通过极化正则化和一次性训练学习共识子网络
abstract: The subject of green AI has been gaining attention within the deep learning community given the recent trend of ever larger and more complex neural network models. Existing solutions for reducing the computational load of training at inference time usually involve pruning the network parameters. Pruning schemes often create extra overhead either by iterative training and fine-tuning for static pruning or repeated computation of a dynamic pruning graph. We propose a new parameter pruning strategy for learning a lighter-weight sub-network that minimizes the energy cost while maintaining comparable performance to the fully parameterised network on given downstream tasks. Our proposed pruning scheme is green-oriented, as it only requires a one-off training to discover the optimal static sub-networks by dynamic pruning methods. The pruning scheme consists of a binary gating module and a polarizing loss function to uncover sub-networks with user-defined sparsity. Our method enables pruning and training simultaneously, which saves energy in both the training and inference phases and avoids extra computational overhead from gating modules at inference time. Our results on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and Tiny Imagenet suggest that our scheme can remove 50% of connections in deep networks with <1% reduction in classification accuracy. Compared to other related pruning methods, our method demonstrates a lower drop in accuracy for equivalent reductions in computational cost.
summary: The subject of green AI has been gaining attention within the deep learning community given the recent trend of ever larger and more complex neural network models. Existing solutions for reducing the computational load of training at inference time usually involve pruning the network parameters. Pruning schemes often create extra overhead either by iterative training and fine-tuning for static pruning or repeated computation of a dynamic pruning graph. We propose a new parameter pruning strategy for learning a lighter-weight sub-network that minimizes the energy cost while maintaining comparable performance to the fully parameterised network on given downstream tasks. Our proposed pruning scheme is green-oriented, as it only requires a one-off training to discover the optimal static sub-networks by dynamic pruning methods. The pruning scheme consists of a binary gating module and a polarizing loss function to uncover sub-networks with user-defined sparsity. Our method enables pruning and training simultaneously, which saves energy in both the training and inference phases and avoids extra computational overhead from gating modules at inference time. Our results on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and Tiny Imagenet suggest that our scheme can remove 50% of connections in deep networks with <1% reduction in classification accuracy. Compared to other related pruning methods, our method demonstrates a lower drop in accuracy for equivalent reductions in computational cost.
abstract_zh: 鉴于最近神经网络模型规模越来越大、越来越复杂的趋势，绿色人工智能这一主题在深度学习社区中越来越受到关注。减少推理时训练计算负载的现有解决方案通常涉及修剪网络参数。剪枝方案通常会通过静态剪枝的迭代训练和微调或动态剪枝图的重复计算来产生额外的开销。我们提出了一种新的参数修剪策略，用于学习更轻量级的子网络，最大限度地减少能源成本，同时在给定的下游任务上保持与完全参数化网络相当的性能。我们提出的剪枝方案是面向绿色的，因为它只需要一次性训练即可通过动态剪枝方法发现最佳静态子网络。剪枝方案由二元门控模块和极化损失函数组成，用于发现具有用户定义的稀疏性的子网络。我们的方法可以同时进行修剪和训练，这可以节省训练和推理阶段的能量，并避免推理时门控模块带来的额外计算开销。我们在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100 和 Tiny Imagenet 上的结果表明，我们的方案可以删除深度网络中 50% 的连接，同时分类精度降低 <1%。与其他相关的剪枝方法相比，我们的方法在计算成本的同等降低下，精度下降幅度较小。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种绿色AI导向的参数剪枝策略，通过一次训练同时实现网络剪枝与训练，以降低计算成本并保持模型性能。
AI.motivation: 随着深度学习模型规模增大，现有剪枝方法存在迭代训练或动态剪枝带来的额外计算开销，亟需更高效的轻量化方案。
AI.method: 设计包含二元门控模块和极化损失函数的剪枝框架，在单次训练中动态发现用户定义稀疏度的子网络，避免推理时的门控计算开销。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10/100和Tiny Imagenet数据集上，该方法可移除50%网络连接且分类准确率下降<1%，相比其他方法在同等计算成本下精度损失更小。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在训练和推理阶段均实现能耗降低，为绿色AI应用提供了计算成本与模型性能间的有效平衡方案。

search_text: Learning a Consensus Sub-Network with Polarization Regularization and One Pass Training 通过极化正则化和一次性训练学习共识子网络 The subject of green AI has been gaining attention within the deep learning community given the recent trend of ever larger and more complex neural network models. Existing solutions for reducing the computational load of training at inference time usually involve pruning the network parameters. Pruning schemes often create extra overhead either by iterative training and fine-tuning for static pruning or repeated computation of a dynamic pruning graph. We propose a new parameter pruning strategy for learning a lighter-weight sub-network that minimizes the energy cost while maintaining comparable performance to the fully parameterised network on given downstream tasks. Our proposed pruning scheme is green-oriented, as it only requires a one-off training to discover the optimal static sub-networks by dynamic pruning methods. The pruning scheme consists of a binary gating module and a polarizing loss function to uncover sub-networks with user-defined sparsity. Our method enables pruning and training simultaneously, which saves energy in both the training and inference phases and avoids extra computational overhead from gating modules at inference time. Our results on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and Tiny Imagenet suggest that our scheme can remove 50% of connections in deep networks with <1% reduction in classification accuracy. Compared to other related pruning methods, our method demonstrates a lower drop in accuracy for equivalent reductions in computational cost. 鉴于最近神经网络模型规模越来越大、越来越复杂的趋势，绿色人工智能这一主题在深度学习社区中越来越受到关注。减少推理时训练计算负载的现有解决方案通常涉及修剪网络参数。剪枝方案通常会通过静态剪枝的迭代训练和微调或动态剪枝图的重复计算来产生额外的开销。我们提出了一种新的参数修剪策略，用于学习更轻量级的子网络，最大限度地减少能源成本，同时在给定的下游任务上保持与完全参数化网络相当的性能。我们提出的剪枝方案是面向绿色的，因为它只需要一次性训练即可通过动态剪枝方法发现最佳静态子网络。剪枝方案由二元门控模块和极化损失函数组成，用于发现具有用户定义的稀疏性的子网络。我们的方法可以同时进行修剪和训练，这可以节省训练和推理阶段的能量，并避免推理时门控模块带来的额外计算开销。我们在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100 和 Tiny Imagenet 上的结果表明，我们的方案可以删除深度网络中 50% 的连接，同时分类精度降低 <1%。与其他相关的剪枝方法相比，我们的方法在计算成本的同等降低下，精度下降幅度较小。 本文提出一种绿色AI导向的参数剪枝策略，通过一次训练同时实现网络剪枝与训练，以降低计算成本并保持模型性能。 Xiaoying Zhi,Varun Babbar,Rundong Liu,Pheobe Sun,Fran Silavong,Ruibo Shi,Sean Moran cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Han Zhong,Jiachen Hu,Yecheng Xue,Tongyang Li,Liwei Wang
title: Provably Efficient Exploration in Quantum Reinforcement Learning with Logarithmic Worst-Case Regret
title_zh: 具有对数最坏情况遗憾的量子强化学习中可证明有效的探索
abstract: While quantum reinforcement learning (RL) has attracted a surge of attention recently, its theoretical understanding is limited. In particular, it remains elusive how to design provably efficient quantum RL algorithms that can address the exploration-exploitation trade-off. To this end, we propose a novel UCRL-style algorithm that takes advantage of quantum computing for tabular Markov decision processes (MDPs) with $S$ states, $A$ actions, and horizon $H$, and establish an $\mathcal{O}(\mathrm{poly}(S, A, H, \log T))$ worst-case regret for it, where $T$ is the number of episodes. Furthermore, we extend our results to quantum RL with linear function approximation, which is capable of handling problems with large state spaces. Specifically, we develop a quantum algorithm based on value target regression (VTR) for linear mixture MDPs with $d$-dimensional linear representation and prove that it enjoys $\mathcal{O}(\mathrm{poly}(d, H, \log T))$ regret. Our algorithms are variants of UCRL/UCRL-VTR algorithms in classical RL, which also leverage a novel combination of lazy updating mechanisms and quantum estimation subroutines. This is the key to breaking the $Ω(\sqrt{T})$-regret barrier in classical RL. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work studying the online exploration in quantum RL with provable logarithmic worst-case regret.
summary: While quantum reinforcement learning (RL) has attracted a surge of attention recently, its theoretical understanding is limited. In particular, it remains elusive how to design provably efficient quantum RL algorithms that can address the exploration-exploitation trade-off. To this end, we propose a novel UCRL-style algorithm that takes advantage of quantum computing for tabular Markov decision processes (MDPs) with $S$ states, $A$ actions, and horizon $H$, and establish an $\mathcal{O}(\mathrm{poly}(S, A, H, \log T))$ worst-case regret for it, where $T$ is the number of episodes. Furthermore, we extend our results to quantum RL with linear function approximation, which is capable of handling problems with large state spaces. Specifically, we develop a quantum algorithm based on value target regression (VTR) for linear mixture MDPs with $d$-dimensional linear representation and prove that it enjoys $\mathcal{O}(\mathrm{poly}(d, H, \log T))$ regret. Our algorithms are variants of UCRL/UCRL-VTR algorithms in classical RL, which also leverage a novel combination of lazy updating mechanisms and quantum estimation subroutines. This is the key to breaking the $Ω(\sqrt{T})$-regret barrier in classical RL. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work studying the online exploration in quantum RL with provable logarithmic worst-case regret.
abstract_zh: 虽然量子强化学习（RL）最近引起了人们的广泛关注，但其理论理解仍然有限。特别是，如何设计可证明有效的量子强化学习算法来解决探索与利用的权衡问题仍然难以捉摸。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的 UCRL 式算法，利用量子计算进行具有 $S$ 状态、$A$ 动作和 $H$ 的表格马尔可夫决策过程 (MDP)，并建立 $\mathcal{O}(\mathrm{poly}(S, A, H, \log T))$ 最坏情况遗憾，其中 $T$ 是情节数。此外，我们将结果扩展到具有线性函数近似的量子强化学习，它能够处理大状态空间的问题。具体来说，我们开发了一种基于值目标回归（VTR）的量子算法，用于具有 $d$ 维线性表示的线性混合 MDP，并证明它享有 $\mathcal{O}(\mathrm{poly}(d, H, \log T))$ 遗憾。我们的算法是经典强化学习中 UCRL/UCRL-VTR 算法的变体，它还利用了惰性更新机制和量子估计子例程的新颖组合。这是打破经典强化学习中$Ω(\sqrt{T})$-regret 障碍的关键。据我们所知，这是第一篇研究量子强化学习在线探索的工作，并具有可证明的对数最坏情况遗憾。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于量子计算的强化学习算法，针对表格型马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）和线性函数逼近场景，实现了对数最坏情况遗憾的高效探索。
AI.motivation: 经典强化学习在处理探索与利用权衡时存在理论局限，而量子强化学习的理论研究尚不充分，亟需设计具有可证明效率的量子算法以突破经典方法的遗憾下界。
AI.method: 提出基于UCRL框架的量子算法，结合懒惰更新机制与量子估计子程序，并扩展至线性混合MDP的值目标回归（VTR）方法，利用量子计算优势优化探索效率。
AI.result: 为表格型MDP建立了$\\mathcal{O}(\\mathrm{poly}(S, A, H, \\log T))$的最坏情况遗憾界，并为线性函数逼近场景证明了$\\mathcal{O}(\\mathrm{poly}(d, H, \\log T))$的遗憾界，突破了经典RL的$Ω(\\sqrt{T})$遗憾屏障。
AI.conclusion: 首次在量子强化学习中实现可证明的对数最坏情况遗憾，展示了量子计算在解决探索难题中的潜力，为大规模状态空间问题提供了新思路。

authors_text: Tuan Nguyen,Salima Mdhaffar,Natalia Tomashenko,Jean-François Bonastre,Yannick Estève
title: Federated Learning for ASR based on Wav2vec 2.0
title_zh: 基于Wav2vec 2.0的ASR联邦学习
abstract: This paper presents a study on the use of federated learning to train an ASR model based on a wav2vec 2.0 model pre-trained by self supervision. Carried out on the well-known TED-LIUM 3 dataset, our experiments show that such a model can obtain, with no use of a language model, a word error rate of 10.92% on the official TED-LIUM 3 test set, without sharing any data from the different users. We also analyse the ASR performance for speakers depending to their participation to the federated learning. Since federated learning was first introduced for privacy purposes, we also measure its ability to protect speaker identity. To do that, we exploit an approach to analyze information contained in exchanged models based on a neural network footprint on an indicator dataset. This analysis is made layer-wise and shows which layers in an exchanged wav2vec 2.0 based model bring the speaker identity information.
summary: This paper presents a study on the use of federated learning to train an ASR model based on a wav2vec 2.0 model pre-trained by self supervision. Carried out on the well-known TED-LIUM 3 dataset, our experiments show that such a model can obtain, with no use of a language model, a word error rate of 10.92% on the official TED-LIUM 3 test set, without sharing any data from the different users. We also analyse the ASR performance for speakers depending to their participation to the federated learning. Since federated learning was first introduced for privacy purposes, we also measure its ability to protect speaker identity. To do that, we exploit an approach to analyze information contained in exchanged models based on a neural network footprint on an indicator dataset. This analysis is made layer-wise and shows which layers in an exchanged wav2vec 2.0 based model bring the speaker identity information.

search_text: Federated Learning for ASR based on Wav2vec 2.0 基于Wav2vec 2.0的ASR联邦学习 This paper presents a study on the use of federated learning to train an ASR model based on a wav2vec 2.0 model pre-trained by self supervision. Carried out on the well-known TED-LIUM 3 dataset, our experiments show that such a model can obtain, with no use of a language model, a word error rate of 10.92% on the official TED-LIUM 3 test set, without sharing any data from the different users. We also analyse the ASR performance for speakers depending to their participation to the federated learning. Since federated learning was first introduced for privacy purposes, we also measure its ability to protect speaker identity. To do that, we exploit an approach to analyze information contained in exchanged models based on a neural network footprint on an indicator dataset. This analysis is made layer-wise and shows which layers in an exchanged wav2vec 2.0 based model bring the speaker identity information. authors_text: George Boateng,Samuel John,Samuel Boateng,Philemon Badu,Patrick Agyeman-Budu,Victor Kumbol
title: Real-World Deployment and Evaluation of Kwame for Science, An AI Teaching Assistant for Science Education in West Africa
title_zh: 西非科学教育人工智能助教 Kwame for Science 的实际部署和评估
comment: 14 pages, Accepted for publication at the 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED 2024)
comments: 14 pages, Accepted for publication at the 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED 2024)
abstract: Africa has a high student-to-teacher ratio which limits students' access to teachers for learning support such as educational question answering. In this work, we extended Kwame, a bilingual AI teaching assistant for coding education, adapted it for science education, and deployed it as a web app. Kwame for Science provides passages from well-curated knowledge sources and related past national exam questions as answers to questions from students based on the Integrated Science subject of the West African Senior Secondary Certificate Examination (WASSCE). Furthermore, students can view past national exam questions along with their answers and filter by year, question type, and topics that were automatically categorized by a topic detection model which we developed (91% unweighted average recall). We deployed Kwame for Science in the real world over 8 months and had 750 users across 32 countries (15 in Africa) and 1.5K questions asked. Our evaluation showed an 87.2% top 3 accuracy (n=109 questions) implying that Kwame for Science has a high chance of giving at least one useful answer among the 3 displayed. We categorized the reasons the model incorrectly answered questions to provide insights for future improvements. We also share challenges and lessons with the development, deployment, and human-computer interaction component of such a tool to enable other researchers to deploy similar tools. With a first-of-its-kind tool within the African context, Kwame for Science has the potential to enable the delivery of scalable, cost-effective, and quality remote education to millions of people across Africa.

In this work, we extended Kwame, a bilingual AI teaching assistant for coding education, adapted it for science education, and deployed it as a web app. Kwame for Science provides passages from well-curated knowledge sources and related past national exam questions as answers to questions from students based on the Integrated Science subject of the West African Senior Secondary Certificate Examination (WASSCE). Furthermore, students can view past national exam questions along with their answers and filter by year, question type, and topics that were automatically categorized by a topic detection model which we developed (91% unweighted average recall). We deployed Kwame for Science in the real world over 8 months and had 750 users across 32 countries (15 in Africa) and 1.5K questions asked. Our evaluation showed an 87.2% top 3 accuracy (n=109 questions) implying that Kwame for Science has a high chance of giving at least one useful answer among the 3 displayed. We categorized the reasons the model incorrectly answered questions to provide insights for future improvements. We also share challenges and lessons with the development, deployment, and human-computer interaction component of such a tool to enable other researchers to deploy similar tools. With a first-of-its-kind tool within the African context, Kwame for Science has the potential to enable the delivery of scalable, cost-effective, and quality remote education to millions of people across Africa.
abstract_zh: 非洲的学生与教师比例较高，这限制了学生向教师寻求学习支持（例如教育问题解答）的机会。在这项工作中，我们扩展了用于编码教育的双语 AI 助教 Kwame，将其改编为科学教育，并将其部署为 Web 应用程序。 Kwame for Science 提供精心策划的知识来源和过去相关国家考试问题的段落，作为学生基于西非高中证书考试 (WASSCE) 综合科学科目的问题的答案。此外，学生可以查看过去的国家考试问题及其答案，并按年份、问题类型和主题进行筛选，这些主题由我们开发的主题检测模型自动分类（91% 的未加权平均召回率）。我们在现实世界中部署了 Kwame for Science 超过 8 个月，拥有来自 32 个国家/地区的 750 名用户（15 个在非洲），并提出了 1,500 个问题。我们的评估显示前 3 个问题的准确率为 87.2%（n=109 个问题），这意味着 Kwame for Science 有很大机会在显示的 3 个答案中至少给出一个有用的答案。我们对模型错误回答问题的原因进行了分类，以便为未来的改进提供见解。我们还分享此类工具的开发、部署和人机交互组件的挑战和经验教训，以便其他研究人员能够部署类似的工具。凭借非洲地区首创的同类工具，Kwame for Science 有潜力为非洲数百万人提供可扩展、经济高效且优质的远程教育。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了Kwame for Science的开发与部署，这是一个针对西非科学教育的双语AI教学助手，通过提供考试题目和知识源内容帮助学生学习。
AI.motivation: 非洲学生与教师比例高，限制了学生获取学习支持的途径。本文旨在通过AI工具弥补教育资源不足，提供可扩展的远程教育方案。
AI.method: 扩展原有Kwame系统以适应科学教育，整合权威知识库和历年考试题，开发自动分类模型（91%召回率），部署为网页应用并进行8个月实地测试。
AI.result: 吸引750名用户（含15个非洲国家），处理1500个问题，top-3答案准确率达87.2%，并分析了错误原因及改进方向。
AI.conclusion: Kwame for Science验证了AI在非洲教育场景的有效性，为规模化、低成本的远程教育提供了可行方案及实施经验。
